  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Project revenue and profitability are reported to profit centers for segment performance reporting.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Project Execution tracks the main stock location for on‑site inventory used by the engineering project.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: PROJECT MANAGEMENT: Engineering projects track a lead supplier for budgeting, risk assessment, and contract governance.']

  eco_count varchar [note: 'Total number of Engineering Change Orders issued during the engineering project.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the engineering project was officially closed or completed.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified the engineering project record.']

  pfmea_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis has been completed for the engineering project.']
  ppap_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Production Part Approval Process is required for the engineering project deliverables.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority ranking assigned to the engineering project for resource allocation and scheduling decisions.']
  program_phase varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle phase of the engineering project within the product development process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|feasibility|design|development|validation|launch|production|closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the engineering project for identification and tracking across systems.']
  project_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the engineering project objectives, scope, and deliverables.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the engineering project describing the initiative or product being developed.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the engineering project indicating its execution state.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the engineering project based on its strategic purpose and scope.']
  prototype_count varchar [note: 'Number of physical or digital prototypes developed as part of the engineering project.']
  regulatory_compliance_scope varchar [note: 'List of regulatory standards and certifications that the engineering project must comply with (e.g., ISO 9001, CE, UL, IEC 61131).']

  start_date date [note: 'Date when the engineering project was officially initiated and work began.']

  target_launch_date date [note: 'Planned date for the engineering project to complete development and transition to production or market launch.']
  target_market_segment varchar [note: 'Primary market segment or customer group that the engineering project is designed to serve.']
  team_size_count varchar [note: 'Number of engineers and team members assigned to the engineering project.']
  technology_platform varchar [note: 'Core technology platform or architecture that the engineering project is based on (e.g., PLC, SCADA, IoT, IIoT).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created the engineering project record.']

  Note: 'Engineering project master record representing a discrete R&D or product development initiative. Captures project code, project name, project type (new product development, product improvement, platform, cost reduction), program phase (concept, development, validation, launch), target launch date, project manager, budget allocation, priority, and linked product family. Serves as the organizing entity for all engineering deliverables, prototypes, and design reviews within a development program.'
}

  acceptance_criteria_lower_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for the test measurement. Defines the lower boundary of the pass/fail acceptance range.']
  acceptance_criteria_target decimal(18,6) [note: 'Target or nominal value for the test measurement. Represents the ideal design specification value within the acceptance range.']
  acceptance_criteria_upper_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for the test measurement. Defines the upper boundary of the pass/fail acceptance range.']
  build_number varchar [note: 'Prototype build iteration number or production batch identifier. Tracks which manufacturing build or prototype iteration the tested unit belongs to.']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory or certification authority for which this test evidence is required (e.g., UL, TUV, CSA, FCC). Applicable when regulatory submission flag is true.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this test result record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']
  dvp_r_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Design Verification Plan and Report document that governs this test. Links test execution to the master validation plan.']
  environmental_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of environmental conditions during test execution (e.g., temperature, humidity, pressure). Captures test environment context for result interpretation.']
  failure_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of any failure, defect, or non-conformance observed during the test. Captures failure mode, symptoms, and conditions. Populated only when test outcome is fail or conditional pass.']
  failure_mode_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code or identifier for the observed failure mode. Links test failure to DFMEA or PFMEA failure mode catalog for root cause analysis.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Primary quantitative measurement or test result value obtained during the test. The principal measured outcome of the test activity.']
  measured_value_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the measured value (e.g., volts, amps, degrees Celsius, newtons, cycles). Ensures correct interpretation of test measurements.']
  measurement_uncertainty decimal(18,6) [note: 'Quantified uncertainty or tolerance associated with the measured value. Expresses the confidence interval or margin of error in the measurement.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this test result record. Audit trail for record changes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this test result record was last modified in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']
  prototype_phase varchar [note: 'Development phase or maturity stage of the tested unit. Indicates whether the unit is a concept prototype, alpha build, beta unit, pre-production sample, or production unit.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test result is required for regulatory certification or compliance submission (e.g., UL, CE, FCC). Flags test data needed for regulatory dossiers.']
  retest_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test is a retest of a previously failed or inconclusive test. Used to track test iteration history.']
  root_cause_analysis_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal root cause analysis (RCA) is required for this test result. Typically true for test failures or unexpected results.']
  test_date date [note: 'Date when the test was executed. Principal business event timestamp for the test activity.']
  test_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time for test execution measured in hours. Captures the actual test run time for endurance and long-duration tests.']
  test_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the test execution completed. Used for duration calculation and detailed test timeline tracking.']
  test_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes, observations, or comments recorded by the test engineer during test execution. Captures contextual information not covered by structured fields.']
  test_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the test execution. Externally-known unique test reference number used for traceability and reporting.']
  test_outcome varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail result of the test execution. Indicates whether the tested unit met the acceptance criteria defined in the test specification.']
  test_purpose varchar [note: 'Business objective or reason for conducting the test. Describes the validation goal, such as design verification, production qualification, regulatory compliance, or failure investigation.']
  test_specification_version varchar [note: 'Version or revision of the test specification document used for this test execution. Ensures traceability to the correct test procedure version.']
  test_standard_reference varchar [note: 'Industry or regulatory standard governing the test procedure. References the specific standard, specification, or protocol followed during test execution (e.g., IEC 61131-2, UL 508, ISO 16750).']
  test_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the test execution began. Used for duration calculation and detailed test timeline tracking.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the test execution. Tracks the test through its workflow from scheduling to completion.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the test performed. Defines the category of validation activity conducted on the unit under test. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: functional|environmental|emc|safety|endurance|performance|reliability|thermal|vibration|shock|humidity|salt_spray|dust|ingress_protection — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created this test result record in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']

  Note: 'Engineering test and validation result record capturing outcomes of design verification and validation (DV&V) activities performed on prototypes, pre-production units, or production samples. Captures test type (functional, environmental, EMC, safety, endurance, performance, reliability), test standard reference, tested unit identification (prototype phase, build number, component revision), test date, test facility, pass/fail outcome, measured values vs acceptance criteria, failure description, linked DFMEA failure mode, and prototype build context (phase, purpose, disposition). Supports DVP&R evidence for PPAP, regulatory certification submissions, and R&D program milestone tracking.'
}

Table engineering.affected_item {
  affected_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the affected_item association']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the Component master record that is affected by the engineering change.']
  eco_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the Engineering Change Order that governs this affected item record.']
  disposition_action varchar [note: 'The required action for existing inventory and work-in-progress of this specific component under this ECO. Belongs to the affected item relationship, not to the ECO globally or the component master, as disposition varies per component per change.']
  effectivity_date date [note: 'The date on which this specific component change becomes effective for use in production. May differ from the ECO-level effectivity date when components within the same ECO have staggered implementation schedules.']
  from_revision varchar [note: 'The current revision level of this component before the engineering change is applied. Captured per affected item because different components on the same ECO may be transitioning from different revision levels.']
  implementation_date date [note: 'Actual date when the engineering change was implemented for this specific component in production systems. Captured per affected item for traceability and PPAP compliance.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Current implementation status of the change for this specific component. Tracked per affected item to allow partial ECO closure when some components are implemented ahead of others.']
  quantity_affected decimal [note: 'The quantity of this component in existing inventory or WIP that is subject to the disposition action. Belongs to the affected item relationship as it is specific to the ECO-component pairing at the time of change.']
  to_revision varchar [note: 'The target revision level of this component after the engineering change is applied. Defines the post-change revision state for this specific component-ECO pairing.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Affected Item relationship between an Engineering Change Order (ECO) and a Component. It captures the formal per-component change record within an ECO — the operational entity that engineers create, manage, and close for each component impacted by a change. Each record links one ECO to one component and carries the disposition instruction, revision transition, effectivity data, and implementation status that exist only in the context of this specific ECO-component pairing. Serves as the SSOT for affected item detail required by ISO 9001 change control, PPAP submissions, and quality audit trails.. Existence Justification: In PLM and engineering change management, an ECO formally governs changes to multiple components simultaneously (e.g., a design change affecting a PLC module, its mounting bracket, and a connector), while a single component can be affected by many ECOs over its lifecycle (initial release, design iterations, material substitutions, regulatory updates). This is a first-class operational relationship — engineers actively create, manage, and close \'Affected Item\' records per ECO-component pair, each carrying disposition instructions, revision transitions, and effectivity data that belong to neither the ECO nor the component alone.'
}

Table quality.inspection_plan {
  inspection_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the inspection plan record. Primary key for the inspection_plan data product in the quality domain.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer‑Specific Inspection Plan process requires linking each inspection_plan to the owning customer_account for contract compliance reporting.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Inspection plans are created for each component to define inspection characteristics; linking ensures traceability from plan to the exact component inspected.']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: APQP/PPAP requires configuration-specific inspection plans — a 480V drive variant has different inspection criteria than a 240V variant. Quality engineers must link plans to the exact product configur']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Planning costs for inspections are budgeted to a cost center; required for inspection budgeting and variance analysis.']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.drawing. Business justification: Inspection plans reference engineering drawings for dimensional characteristics and GD&T callouts. This is a fundamental industrial manufacturing practice — inspection plans cite the governing drawing']
  eco_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: When an ECO changes a component, the associated inspection plan must be revised. The existing eco_number plain-text field is a denormalized ECO reference; replacing it with eco_id FK enforces referent']
  engineering_revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. Business justification: Inspection plans must be updated when engineering revisions change part tolerances or specifications. Linking inspection_plan to engineering_revision is a core APQP/PPAP traceability requirement — any']
  engineering_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: Inspection plans are derived directly from engineering specifications defining tolerances, acceptance criteria, and test methods. APQP and PPAP require inspection plans to reference the governing engi']
  fmea_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.fmea. Business justification: inspection_plan carries fmea_reference as a plain STRING field referencing the FMEA that drove the inspection plan\'s characteristic definitions and control limits. This is a denormalized text referenc']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: Inspection plans are plant-specific in SAP QM and industrial QMS systems. inspection_plan.plant_code is a denormalized plain text reference to the plant. Proper FK enables plant-level inspection plan ']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Inspection plans are defined per product SKU for quality control; linking enables plan retrieval by SKU in the Inspection Planning process.']
  sla_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_agreement. Business justification: Customer-specific inspection plans in industrial manufacturing are contractually mandated by SLA agreements specifying AQL levels, PPAP requirements, and APQP phases. Linking inspection_plan to sla_ag']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier to whom this inspection plan applies, for incoming or supplier-specific inspection plans. Relevant for receiving inspection and supplier quality audit plans managed via SAP Ariba.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection plan was formally approved for use. Provides an audit trail for ISO 9001 document control and regulatory compliance.']
  apqp_phase varchar [note: 'APQP phase during which this inspection plan was developed or is applicable. Phase 1: Plan and Define; Phase 2: Product Design and Development; Phase 3: Process Design and Development; Phase 4: Product and Process Validation; Phase 5: Feedback, Assessment and Corrective Action.']
  aql_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable Quality Level expressed as a percentage defective, defining the maximum tolerable defect rate for the sampling plan. Used in conjunction with ISO 2859-1 / ANSI Z1.4 sampling tables.']
  control_method_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the measurement or control method used during inspection (e.g., visual, dimensional, functional, chemical, SPC chart). References the SAP QM master inspection characteristic control indicator.']
  control_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Document number of the associated APQP Control Plan that this inspection plan implements. The control plan defines the process controls and reaction plans linked to inspection characteristics.']
  cpk_minimum decimal(5,3) [note: 'Minimum acceptable Cpk (Process Capability Index) value required for this inspection characteristic. Typically 1.33 for standard processes and 1.67 for safety-critical characteristics per PPAP requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection plan record was first created in the source system. Provides audit trail for data governance and ISO 9001 document control.']
  customer_specific_requirement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection plan was created or modified to satisfy a specific customer quality requirement (CSR) beyond standard internal or regulatory requirements. Relevant for OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) supply relationships.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this inspection plan version becomes valid and applicable for production inspection operations. Aligns with SAP QM validity start date.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which this inspection plan version expires and is no longer valid for new inspection lots. Null indicates the plan is open-ended with no scheduled expiry. Aligns with SAP QM validity end date.']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'Category of measurement equipment or test instrument required to perform the inspection. Used for calibration scheduling and resource allocation in Maximo Asset Management.']
  inspection_method_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the standardized inspection method or test procedure document used to perform the inspection. Links to the SAP QM inspection method master record.']
  inspection_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the intensity of inspection applied: full (normal), reduced (lower frequency for trusted suppliers/processes), tightened (increased scrutiny after failures), or skip-lot (periodic sampling). Aligned with SPC-driven dynamic inspection level adjustments.']
  inspection_stage varchar [note: 'Stage in the production or supply chain process at which the inspection is performed. Incoming covers receiving inspection; in-process covers WIP (Work In Progress) checks; final covers end-of-line; outgoing covers pre-shipment; skip indicates skip-lot logic.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection plan record was last updated in the source system. Used for change tracking, data lineage, and incremental lakehouse ingestion.']
  long_text_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the inspection plan scope, special instructions, safety precautions, and any additional context required by the inspector. Corresponds to SAP QM long text on the task list header.']
  lower_tolerance_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower specification limit for the primary inspection characteristic. Values below this limit result in a non-conformance. Used in SPC and Cp/Cpk capability index calculations.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Date by which the inspection plan must be reviewed and revalidated to ensure continued suitability and compliance with current product specifications and regulatory requirements.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'Routing operation number within the inspection plan, corresponding to the SAP QM task list operation (PLPO.VORNR). Defines the sequence of inspection steps within the plan.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or short description of the inspection plan, identifying the product, process, or operation it governs.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the inspection plan, aligned with SAP QM plan group and plan group counter. Used for cross-system reference and document control.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inspection plan. Controls whether the plan is available for use in production inspection operations. Draft plans are under development; active plans are approved for use; obsolete plans are superseded.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection plan by its operational purpose. Covers in-process, final inspection, receiving inspection, supplier audit, PPAP (Production Part Approval Process), APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning), and first article inspection types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_process|final_inspection|receiving|supplier_audit|ppap|apqp|first_article — promote to reference product]']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version or revision number of the inspection plan, corresponding to the SAP QM plan group counter. Tracks engineering changes and revisions aligned with ECO/ECN processes.']
  ppap_level varchar [note: 'PPAP submission level (1–5) required for this inspection plan, per AIAG PPAP standard. Level 1 requires only a Part Submission Warrant; Level 3 is the default full submission; Level 5 requires records reviewed at the manufacturing site.']
  product_group_code varchar [note: 'SAP material group or product family code to which this inspection plan applies. Enables grouping of inspection plans by product category for reporting and analytics.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for the current plan version, such as an ECO (Engineering Change Order), CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) outcome, customer complaint, or regulatory update.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units to be drawn from the inspection lot for evaluation. Determined by the sampling procedure and AQL level. A value of zero indicates 100% inspection.']
  sample_size_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the sample size quantity (e.g., EA for each/piece, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure for the material. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|M|L|M2|M3 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sampling_procedure_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the statistical sampling procedure applied in this inspection plan, including sample size, acceptance number, and rejection number per AQL (Acceptable Quality Level) tables.']
  spc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SPC (Statistical Process Control) charting and Cp/Cpk capability monitoring are activated for this inspection plan. When true, measurement results feed into control charts and process capability analysis.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Nominal or target value for the primary inspection characteristic. Represents the ideal measurement around which tolerances are defined. Used as the centerline reference in SPC control charts.']
  upper_tolerance_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper specification limit for the primary inspection characteristic. Values exceeding this limit result in a non-conformance. Used in SPC (Statistical Process Control) and Cp/Cpk capability index calculations.']
  usage_decision_code varchar [note: 'SAP QM usage decision code that defines the default disposition action (accept, reject, rework) applied when inspection results meet or fail acceptance criteria.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or production cell where the inspection operation is performed. Corresponds to SAP PP/QM work center assignment on the inspection routing.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the quality engineer or planner who originally created this inspection plan record in SAP QM or the source system.']

  Note: 'Master definition of inspection plans aligned with SAP QM, specifying inspection characteristics, sampling procedures, control methods, and acceptance criteria for in-process and final inspection operations. Covers PPAP, APQP, and routine production inspection plans per ISO 9001 requirements.'
}

Table quality.inspection_lot {
  inspection_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a single inspection lot record in the quality execution system. Primary key for the inspection_lot entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer Inspection Lot Reporting mandates associating each inspection_lot with the customer_account to deliver lot‑level quality results to the customer.']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: Inspection lots must be traceable to the specific product configuration being produced for defect analysis by configuration variant. Quality reporting on defect rates per configuration (e.g., specific']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost of inspection activities allocated to cost center for cost‑of‑quality reporting; finance tracks these expenses.']
  delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.delivery. Business justification: Outbound delivery inspections and inbound goods-receipt inspection lots are directly triggered by a delivery in SAP QM. Quality teams need delivery_id on inspection_lot to trace which shipment trigger']
  goods_issue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.goods_issue. Business justification: In SAP QM and industrial manufacturing, a goods issue movement can trigger an outbound inspection lot (e.g., final inspection before shipment). Linking inspection_lot to goods_issue enables quality ga']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales order associated with this inspection lot, applicable for customer-specific inspection requirements, pre-shipment inspections, or RMA receipt inspections. Links quality execution to order fulfillment.']
  inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inspection plan (control plan) that defines the inspection operations, characteristics, sampling procedures, and acceptance criteria to be executed for this lot. Aligns with SAP QM inspection plan assignment.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: In SAP QM and IATF 16949 manufacturing, every inspection lot is raised against a specific lot/batch. This link enables lot-level quality traceability for PPAP, containment, and disposition decisions. ']
  material_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_requirement. Business justification: Inspection lots for incoming goods trace back to the material requirement that drove the purchase order, enabling planning-to-quality traceability reporting. In industrial manufacturing MRP/QM integra']
  ncr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) record created as a result of this inspection lot\'s non-conformance or reject disposition. Enables traceability from inspection event to corrective action workflow.']
  planned_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.planned_order. Business justification: In MRP-driven manufacturing, inspection lots for incoming goods or production outputs are traceable to the planned order that initiated the requirement. Quality-to-planning traceability reports and MR']
  po_line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.po_line_item. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, incoming inspection lots are created at the PO line item level (each line item may have different material, quantity, and quality requirements). Line-item-level lot tracea']
  primary_inspection_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or part master record subject to this inspection lot. Links to the product/material master in SAP MM/PLM for BOM and specification retrieval.']
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the goods receipt document (SAP material document) that triggered this incoming inspection lot. Applicable for inspection types 01 (GR from PO) and 04 (GR from Production Order). Enables traceability from inspection to inventory movement.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: Inspection lots are created for production on specific lines; line-level quality performance reporting and SPC require knowing which production line generated the lot. Essential for line-level first-p']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing/production order that triggered this inspection lot, applicable for in-process and final inspection types. Links quality execution to production scheduling in SAP PP / Siemens Opcenter MES.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order associated with this inspection lot, applicable for incoming goods receipt inspections from suppliers. Links to SAP MM purchasing documents.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_rma. Business justification: Returned goods under an RMA require a dedicated inspection lot in industrial manufacturing (incoming inspection of returned material). The inspection_lot table carries a denormalized rma_number plain ']
  run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.run. Business justification: Inspection lots are created per production run in process and discrete manufacturing. Run-level quality disposition and traceability require direct linkage. Enables run-level quality yield reporting a']
  serialized_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: For serialized industrial products (drives, PLCs, switchgear), inspection lots are raised against individual serial numbers. Serial-level inspection traceability is required for warranty, field servic']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Inspection lot must be linked to the physical storage location for traceability in the warehouse inspection process, required by ISO 9001 audit.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor master record from whom the material was received, applicable for incoming inspection lots. Enables supplier quality performance tracking and audit.']
  vendor_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Incoming inspection lots in industrial manufacturing originate from a specific supplier site. Site-level quality performance tracking (PPM by site, AQL pass rates by site) is a standard SQE activity a']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center or production cell where the in-process inspection is performed. Applicable for in-process and milestone inspection types. Links to SAP PP work center master.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the quality certificate or Certificate of Conformance (CoC) issued for this inspection lot upon acceptance. Supports customer documentation requirements and regulatory compliance traceability.']
  certificate_of_conformance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Certificate of Conformance (CoC) or quality certificate must be generated and issued upon acceptance of this inspection lot. Driven by customer contract requirements or regulatory mandates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this inspection lot record was first persisted in the data platform (Silver layer). Used for data lineage and audit trail compliance.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Total number of defects or non-conformances detected across all inspection characteristics within this lot. Used for defect rate calculation, SPC analysis, and NCR triggering thresholds.']
  disposition_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the quality engineer or authorized personnel who recorded the usage decision and disposition for this inspection lot. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'SAP QM usage decision code (e.g., A1=Accept, R1=Reject, R2=Rework, S1=Scrap) providing a standardized coded classification of the disposition decision for system processing and reporting.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision made upon completion of inspection results evaluation. Determines the fate of the inspected material: \'accept\' = material released to stock/use; \'reject\' = material blocked/returned; \'rework\' = material sent for corrective rework; \'scrap\' = material scrapped; \'conditional_release\' = accepted with documented deviations/concessions.']
  disposition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the usage decision (disposition) was formally recorded in the quality management system. Marks the closure of the active inspection phase and triggers downstream stock posting or NCR creation.']
  dynamic_modification_rule varchar [note: 'Code or name of the dynamic modification rule applied to automatically adjust the inspection level (normal/tightened/reduced/skip) based on cumulative quality history for this material-supplier or material-plant combination. Aligns with SAP QM dynamic modification.']
  inspection_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when all inspection activities and results recording were completed for this lot. Combined with inspection_start_timestamp to compute inspection cycle time (Lead Time metric).']
  inspection_level varchar [note: 'Current inspection severity level applied to this lot based on the supplier or process quality history. \'normal\' = standard AQL sampling; \'tightened\' = increased scrutiny due to recent failures; \'reduced\' = relaxed sampling due to consistent quality; \'skip\' = lot accepted without inspection based on skip-lot qualification.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Primary inspection method applied to this lot: \'visual\' = visual examination; \'dimensional\' = measurement against dimensional tolerances; \'functional\' = operational/performance testing; \'destructive\' = destructive testing (sample consumed); \'non_destructive\' = NDT methods (ultrasonic, X-ray, etc.); \'chemical\' = chemical composition analysis; \'electrical\' = electrical parameter testing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visual|dimensional|functional|destructive|non_destructive|chemical|electrical — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspection_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when physical inspection activities commenced for this lot. Used to calculate inspection cycle time and monitor SLA compliance for quality turnaround.']
  inspection_type_code varchar [note: 'SAP QM inspection type code classifying the origin and nature of the inspection lot. Standard SAP codes: 01=Goods Receipt from Purchase Order (incoming), 04=Goods Receipt from Production Order (final), 05=Goods Issue, 06=Delivery to Customer, 08=In-Process Inspection, 10=Recurring Inspection, 89=RMA/Customer Return Receipt. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|04|05|06|08|10|89 — promote to reference product for full SAP inspection type catalogue]']
  inspection_type_description varchar [note: 'Business-readable classification of the inspection event type: \'incoming\' = supplier goods receipt; \'in_process\' = mid-production milestone check; \'final\' = post-production completion check; \'recurring\' = periodic scheduled inspection; \'rma_receipt\' = customer return receipt inspection; \'delivery\' = pre-shipment inspection; \'goods_issue\' = outbound goods inspection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: incoming|in_process|final|recurring|rma_receipt|delivery|goods_issue — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the inspection lot by the quality management system (e.g., SAP QM lot number). Used for cross-system traceability and communication with shop floor, suppliers, and auditors.']
  lot_origin_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world business event timestamp recording when the inspection lot was triggered and created in the quality management system. Distinct from ETL audit timestamps. Aligns with SAP QM lot creation date/time.']
  lot_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of material or units submitted for inspection in this lot. Represents the population from which samples are drawn. Expressed in the base unit of measure of the material.']
  lot_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the inspection lot quantity (e.g., EA=Each, KG=Kilogram, M=Meter, L=Liter, M2=Square Meter). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure from material master.']
  lot_remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for quality engineers to record observations, special conditions, deviations, or contextual notes relevant to this inspection lot that are not captured in structured fields. Supports audit documentation.']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the inspection lot within the quality execution lifecycle. Drives downstream actions: \'created\' = lot opened awaiting release; \'released\' = inspection authorized to begin; \'results_recorded\' = all characteristic results entered; \'usage_decided\' = disposition decision made; \'closed\' = lot archived. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|released|results_recorded|usage_decided|closed — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  ncr_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Non-Conformance Report (NCR) was automatically or manually triggered as a result of this inspection lot\'s reject disposition or detected non-conformances. True = NCR created; False = no NCR required.']
  nonconforming_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units or material within the lot that failed to meet specification requirements. Expressed in the same unit of measure as lot_quantity. Used to calculate reject rate and determine disposition scope.']
  overall_result varchar [note: 'Summarized quality outcome of the inspection lot based on all characteristic results evaluated against acceptance criteria. \'passed\' = all characteristics within specification; \'failed\' = one or more characteristics out of specification; \'conditionally_passed\' = passed with documented deviations; \'pending\' = results not yet fully recorded.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or site where the inspection lot was created and executed. Supports multi-plant quality reporting and compliance.']
  required_end_date date [note: 'Target date by which the inspection lot must be completed and a usage decision recorded, as defined by the inspection plan or production schedule. Supports SLA monitoring and escalation management.']
  revision_level varchar [note: 'Engineering revision or drawing revision level of the material or part at the time of inspection. Ensures inspection results are traceable to the correct design revision, supporting Engineering Change Order (ECO) and ECN management.']
  sample_drawing_procedure varchar [note: 'Code or name of the sampling procedure applied to determine the sample size for this inspection lot (e.g., AQL 1.0, AQL 2.5, 100% inspection, skip-lot). Derived from the inspection plan sampling scheme.']
  sample_size decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual number of units or quantity drawn from the lot for physical inspection, as determined by the sampling procedure in the inspection plan. May differ from the planned sample size if adjustments were made.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this inspection lot record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports change detection and incremental processing.']

  Note: 'Transactional inspection lot record representing a single inspection event triggered by goods receipt, production order completion, in-process milestone, periodic schedule, or customer return receipt. Captures lot origin, material/part reference, inspection quantity, inspection type (incoming, in-process, final, recurring, RMA-receipt), current status (created, released, results-recorded, usage-decided), and disposition decision (accept, reject, rework, scrap). Links to inspection plan for execution instructions and generates inspection_result child records. Triggers NCR creation upon reject disposition or non-conformance detection. Central transactional entity for quality execution aligned with SAP QM inspection lot processing.'
}

Table quality.inspection_result {
  inspection_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each inspection characteristic result record within an inspection lot. Primary key for the inspection_result data product in the quality domain.']
  calibration_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.calibration_record. Business justification: ISO 9001/IATF 16949 require measurement results to be traceable to the calibrated instrument used. Linking inspection_result to the specific calibration_record used during measurement is a mandatory m']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the measurement equipment or gauge used to capture the inspection result. Supports gauge R&R studies and calibration traceability per MSA requirements.']
  inspection_characteristic_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master inspection characteristic definition (e.g., dimension, hardness, visual attribute) as configured in the inspection plan. Enables aggregation of results across lots for SPC analysis.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent inspection lot under which this characteristic result was recorded. Links the result to its originating inspection event in SAP QM.']
  inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inspection plan (control plan) that defines the characteristic being measured. Ties the result back to the approved quality plan and sampling procedure.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Inspection results must be traceable to the specific lot/batch for PPAP documentation, SPC analysis by batch, and customer-specific traceability requirements (IATF 16949). batch_number on inspection_r']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or product being inspected. Enables quality performance analysis by SKU, part number, or finished good across production runs.']
  ncr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.ncr. Business justification: inspection_result currently carries ncr_number as a plain STRING reference to the NCR triggered by an out-of-spec or out-of-control measurement. This is a denormalized text reference that should be re']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Required for linking each inspection result to its specific order line, enabling quality reports and order fulfillment decisions.']
  product_revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_revision. Business justification: PPAP submissions require inspection results traceable to the specific product revision inspected. revision_level is a denormalized plain attribute on inspection_result; replacing it with a proper FK t']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing production order associated with the inspected batch or lot. Enables traceability from quality result back to the production execution event.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_order. Business justification: Required for Supplier Performance Report linking inspection results to the purchase order that supplied the material, enabling root‑cause analysis per supplier.']
  run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.run. Business justification: Run‑level quality reports aggregate inspection results per production run for OEE and compliance dashboards.']
  serialized_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: End-of-line test results for serialized automation equipment must link to the specific serialized unit for warranty claims, field failure analysis, and customer-specific serial traceability. serial_nu']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Inspection results are recorded for each inspected product SKU; linking allows quality dashboards to aggregate results by SKU.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing work center or production cell where the inspection was performed. Supports OEE and quality-by-location analysis.']
  attribute_result varchar [note: 'Pass/fail judgment for attribute-type inspection characteristics (e.g., visual defect present/absent, functional test pass/fail). Null for variable characteristics where measured_value is populated.']
  cp_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Process capability index Cp measuring the ratio of the specification width to the process spread (6-sigma). Indicates whether the process is capable of meeting specification limits regardless of centering. Cp ≥ 1.33 is typically required per PPAP.']
  cpk_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Process capability index Cpk measuring the ratio of the nearest specification limit to the process mean, accounting for process centering. Cpk ≥ 1.33 is typically required for PPAP approval. Key metric for supplier quality and process performance reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection result record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking per lakehouse governance requirements.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized defect classification code identifying the type of nonconformance detected (e.g., DIM-OOT for out-of-tolerance dimension, SURF-SCR for surface scratch). Feeds Pareto analysis and CAPA root cause categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for defect code catalog]']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Number of defects or nonconformances detected for this characteristic within the inspected sample. Used for defect rate calculation, Pareto analysis, and NCR triggering thresholds.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the nonconformance or defect observed during inspection. Provides context beyond the defect code for root cause analysis and CAPA documentation.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the inspection was performed. Used for daily/weekly quality trend reporting and shift-level aggregation without requiring timestamp precision.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the inspection: \'manual\' (operator measurement), \'automated\' (CMM or inline sensor), \'semi_automated\', \'destructive\' (material consumed in test), or \'non_destructive\' (NDT methods such as ultrasonic, X-ray). Affects result reliability and sampling plan selection.']
  inspection_stage varchar [note: 'Stage in the production or supply chain process at which the inspection was performed: \'incoming\' (goods receipt), \'in_process\' (during manufacturing), \'final\' (finished goods), \'outgoing\' (pre-shipment), or \'supplier\' (at supplier site).']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection measurement was physically recorded. The principal business event timestamp for this result. Used for SPC time-series analysis, shift-based quality reporting, and audit trail.']
  is_out_of_control boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measurement triggered an SPC out-of-control signal (e.g., point beyond control limits, run rule violation per Western Electric rules). Distinct from out-of-spec: a process can be in-spec but out-of-control. Triggers SPC investigation workflow.']
  is_out_of_spec boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measured value falls outside the engineering specification limits (USL or LSL). True when measured_value > upper_spec_limit or measured_value < lower_spec_limit. Used for rapid filtering of nonconforming results and NCR triggering.']
  lower_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lower statistical process control limit for the characteristic, typically set at ±3 sigma from the process mean. Used in SPC X-bar and R charts to detect process shifts.']
  lower_spec_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lower engineering specification limit for the characteristic. Measurements below this value are out-of-specification. Used in Cp/Cpk calculation and SPC control chart setup.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual numeric measurement recorded for a variable inspection characteristic (e.g., 24.987 mm for a diameter). Null for attribute-type characteristics. Core data point for SPC charting and Cp/Cpk calculation.']
  nominal_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The engineering design target or nominal value for the characteristic as specified in the drawing or specification. Used to calculate deviation from target and for SPC centering analysis.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the inspection was performed. Enables multi-plant quality benchmarking and regulatory reporting by site.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks or observations entered by the inspector at the time of measurement. Captures contextual information not covered by structured fields, such as environmental conditions, equipment anomalies, or visual observations.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Current disposition status of the inspection characteristic result: \'accepted\' (within specification), \'rejected\' (out of specification), \'conditional\' (requires further review or deviation approval), \'open\' (not yet evaluated), or \'cancelled\'.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units or specimens included in the inspection sample for this characteristic result. Determined by the sampling plan (e.g., AQL per ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 or Z1.9). Required for statistical validity assessment.']
  sampling_procedure varchar [note: 'Sampling procedure or plan applied for this inspection characteristic (e.g., AQL 1.0 Level II, 100% inspection, skip-lot). Determines the statistical confidence of the inspection result.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Production shift during which the inspection was performed (e.g., day, afternoon, night). Enables shift-based quality performance analysis to identify systematic variation by shift.']
  spc_chart_type varchar [note: 'Type of SPC control chart applied to this characteristic: \'xbar_r\' (X-bar and R for subgroups), \'i_mr\' (Individuals and Moving Range), \'p_chart\' (proportion defective), \'np_chart\' (number defective), \'c_chart\' (count of defects), \'u_chart\' (defects per unit). Determines the statistical method for control limit calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: xbar_r|xbar_s|i_mr|p_chart|np_chart|c_chart|u_chart — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  subgroup_number varchar [note: 'Sequential subgroup number within the SPC control chart for this characteristic. Used to order data points on the SPC chart and identify the rational subgroup to which this measurement belongs.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Engineering unit of the measured value (e.g., mm, kg, MPa, Ra, °C, %). Follows ISO 80000 and SAP UoM conventions. Required for dimensional analysis and SPC chart labeling.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this inspection result record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  upper_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The upper statistical process control limit for the characteristic, typically set at ±3 sigma from the process mean. Triggers investigation when exceeded even if within specification. Used in SPC X-bar and R charts.']
  upper_spec_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The upper engineering specification limit for the characteristic. Measurements above this value are out-of-specification. Used in Cp/Cpk calculation and SPC control chart setup.']
  usage_decision_code varchar [note: 'SAP QM usage decision code indicating the final disposition of the inspected material based on this and other characteristic results: \'accept\' (release to stock), \'reject\' (return or scrap), \'rework\' (send for rework), \'scrap\', or \'conditional_release\' (deviation approval). Drives inventory posting in SAP MM.']

  Note: 'Detailed measurement and characteristic result records captured during inspection lot execution. Stores individual measured values, attribute results (pass/fail), SPC data points, Cp/Cpk indices, and defect codes per inspection characteristic. Feeds SPC charts and process capability reporting.'
}

Table quality.ncr {
  ncr_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) record. Primary key for the quality event.']

  account_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: NCRs triggered by customer-reported nonconformances are tied to the specific customer site where nonconforming product is installed or received.   cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Non‑conformance remediation costs are charged to a cost center; required for cost of poor quality analysis.']
  delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.delivery. Business justification: NCRs in industrial manufacturing are frequently raised against a specific delivery (damaged goods on arrival, wrong items shipped). Linking ncr to delivery enables direct traceability from nonconforma']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: ECNs are issued in response to quality NCRs when design changes are required. Quality teams reference the ECN that resolved the nonconformance for regulatory audit traceability and PPAP documentation ']
  eco_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: NCRs in industrial manufacturing frequently trigger or reference an ECO to address root causes at the design level. Linking NCR to ECO enables closed-loop quality-to-engineering change traceability re']
  engineering_revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_revision. This is standard practice i']
  engineering_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: NCRs document which engineering specification was violated. Linking ncr to engineering_specification enables specification-level nonconformance analysis and supports regulatory compliance reporting — ']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: Allows NCR investigations to identify the exact equipment causing non‑conformance, required for root‑cause analysis reports.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or facility where the non-conformance was detected or originated. Aligns with SAP plant organizational unit.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: NCRs in industrial manufacturing are raised against specific lots/batches for quarantine, disposition, and regulatory traceability (ISO 9001, IATF 16949). Lot-level NCR tracking drives containment sco']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material, component, or finished product involved in the non-conformance. Maps to SAP MM material master.']
  material_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_requirement. Business justification: When an NCR results in batch rejection, the material requirement that drove the procurement must be flagged so MRP can re-plan the net requirement. Industrial manufacturing MRP exception management de']

Linking NCR to order_intake enables RMA processing, customer notification, disposition tracking, and order-level nonconfor']

  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: NCRs are raised against specific work orders for nonconforming production output. Direct FK enables work-order-level nonconformance reporting, cost of quality analysis, and scrap/rework traceability —']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_order. Business justification: Needed for NCR Traceability Report to associate non‑conformances with the originating purchase order for supplier corrective actions.']
  quality_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_audit. Business justification: A quality audit (internal, supplier, or process audit) frequently generates NCRs as findings. quality_audit tracks major_ncr_count and minor_ncr_count as aggregate counts, but there is no FK from indi']
  serialized_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit.
