  converted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the planned order was converted to an executable order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this planned order record was first created in the system by the planning run.']
  deletion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that this planned order has been marked for deletion by a subsequent planning run because the requirement no longer exists or has been satisfied by other means.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying planning exceptions or issues requiring planner intervention (e.g., capacity overload, material shortage, lead time violation, MOQ conflict).']
  exception_message varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the planning exception or constraint violation that requires planner review and resolution.']
  firmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the planned order was firmed by the planner.']
  firming_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this planned order has been firmed by the planner, preventing it from being automatically changed or deleted by subsequent planning runs.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this planned order record was last updated, either by a subsequent planning run or by manual planner intervention.']
  lot_size_rule varchar [note: 'Lot sizing method applied by the planning system: exact_quantity (order exact requirement), moq (minimum order quantity), fixed_lot (standard batch size), or rounding (round up to packaging unit).']
  moq_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum order quantity constraint from the supplier or production process that was applied during lot sizing.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'Code identifying the planner or planning group responsible for reviewing and converting this planned order.']
  multi_tier_supplier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that this planned order requires coordination across multiple tiers of the supply network (e.g., raw material supplier, component manufacturer, final assembly).']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Type of replenishment action proposed by the planning system: production order for in-house manufacturing, purchase requisition for external procurement, stock transfer for inter-plant movement, or subcontract order for external processing.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material proposed for replenishment by the planning run, expressed in base unit of measure.']
  planner_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text comments entered by the planner documenting decisions, constraints, or coordination details related to this planned order.']
  planner_override_date date [note: 'Manually adjusted scheduled finish date entered by the planner to override the system-calculated date based on capacity constraints or customer negotiation.']
  planner_override_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Manually adjusted quantity entered by the planner to override the system-calculated planned quantity based on business judgment or known constraints.']
  planning_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the planning run that created this planned order was executed.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Planning priority assigned to this order based on demand urgency, customer importance, or supply risk. Critical orders require immediate planner attention.']
  procurement_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to procure or produce the material, used by the planning system to calculate scheduled start date from requirement date.']
  proposal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the planned order proposal: open (awaiting planner review), under_review (being evaluated), firmed (approved for conversion), converted (released to execution), rejected (not needed), or cancelled (superseded by new planning run).']
  required_capacity_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Production capacity in hours required to complete this planned order based on routing and quantity.']
  requirement_date date [note: 'Date when the material is needed to fulfill a sales order, production order component, or safety stock replenishment.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Buffer stock level maintained to protect against demand variability or supply disruptions, factored into the planning calculation.']
  scheduled_finish_date date [note: 'Planned date when the material should be available to satisfy the downstream requirement or customer demand.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date when production or procurement activities should begin to meet the requirement date, calculated backward from finish date using lead time.']
  source_requirement_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the originating requirement (sales order, production order, forecast entry) that drove the creation of this planned order.']
  source_requirement_type varchar [note: 'Type of demand that triggered the creation of this planned order: sales order (customer demand), production order (component requirement), safety stock (replenishment), forecast (anticipated demand), or service order (spare parts).']
  supply_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk assessment score (0-100) indicating the likelihood of supply disruption for this planned order based on supplier performance, lead time volatility, and geopolitical factors.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the planned quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter).']

  Note: 'Represents a planned production, procurement, or stock transfer order generated by MRP/APS to satisfy a material requirement. Consolidates all replenishment proposals awaiting planner review through to firmed orders ready for conversion. Tracks planned quantity, scheduled start/finish dates, order type (production order, purchase requisition, stock transfer), priority, firming status, proposal lifecycle (open, under review, firmed, converted, rejected), planner override details, converting planner ID, and the material/plant combination. Serves as the single actionable output of MRP runs requiring human review before release to procurement or production. Sourced from SAP PP/MM and Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM planning outputs.'
}

Table supply.demand_forecast {
  demand_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand forecast record. Primary key for the demand forecast entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer‑specific demand forecasts are generated per account to support sales‑aligned planning and KPI reporting.']
  account_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Industrial manufacturers forecast demand at the customer ship-to site level for large accounts with multiple plants. Site-level demand forecasting drives logistics capacity planning and inventory posi']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: In configure-to-order industrial manufacturing, demand forecasts are created at the configuration level (e.g., specific voltage/power variant of a drive system) to support S&OP and ATP processes. Plan']
  demand_plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the forecast version record enabling scenario comparison and version lineage tracking.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Demand forecasts in industrial manufacturing are generated at the product family level using statistical models, then disaggregated to SKU. This FK supports family-level forecast accuracy reporting, S']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or item being forecasted. Links to the material master record in inventory domain.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: S&OP process in industrial manufacturing explicitly reconciles demand forecasts against the sales opportunity pipeline. Planners need to trace which opportunity seeded a forecast entry for pipeline-ba']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Demand forecasting in industrial manufacturing is structured by customer segment (OEM, distributor, process industry). Planners generate and review segment-level forecasts for capacity and supply plan']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Forecasting uses the SKU master to align demand quantities with product specifications and pricing.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the planning location, warehouse, or distribution center for which demand is forecasted.']
  approval_comments varchar [note: 'Comments or notes provided by the approver during the forecast approval workflow, documenting decision rationale or conditions.']
  bias_percent decimal(6,2) [note: 'Systematic forecast bias expressed as percentage, indicating tendency to over-forecast (positive) or under-forecast (negative). Used to detect and correct systematic errors.']
  confidence_interval_lower decimal(18,3) [note: 'Lower bound of the forecast confidence interval, representing the pessimistic demand scenario for risk analysis and safety stock planning.']
  confidence_interval_upper decimal(18,3) [note: 'Upper bound of the forecast confidence interval, representing the optimistic demand scenario for capacity planning and supply risk assessment.']
  confidence_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level of the forecast interval expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00 for 95% confidence). Used for supply planning sensitivity analysis.']
  consensus_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the forecast achieved consensus approval in the Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) process, marking it as the agreed demand plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  demand_class varchar [note: 'Classification of demand type: independent (end-customer driven, forecasted) or dependent (derived from BOM and parent item demand via MRP explosion).']
  demand_pattern varchar [note: 'Characterization of the historical demand behavior pattern used to select appropriate forecasting models and safety stock strategies.']
  forecast_accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical accuracy percentage of the forecast model for this material-location combination, calculated as (1 - MAPE) * 100. Used for model selection and tuning.']
  forecast_consumption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forecast is subject to consumption by actual customer orders in the MRP logic. Controls forecast vs. order-driven planning behavior.']
  forecast_generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the forecast was generated by the forecasting engine or planning system. Represents the business event time of forecast creation.']
  forecast_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days into the future that this forecast covers, calculated from the planning period start date. Used for lead time coverage analysis.']
  forecast_model_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the statistical or algorithmic model used to generate the forecast (e.g., ARIMA, EXP_SMOOTH, MOVING_AVG, ML_ENSEMBLE).']
  forecast_model_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the forecasting model or algorithm applied to generate this forecast.']
  forecast_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the forecast record, externally visible and used for tracking and reference in planning meetings.']
  forecast_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Predicted demand quantity for the material at the specified location during the planning period. Core forecast value used for MRP and capacity planning.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the forecast record in the approval workflow. Tracks progression from draft through approval or rejection.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this forecast record, tracking manual adjustments or workflow updates.']
  mean_absolute_percentage_error decimal(5,2) [note: 'MAPE metric measuring forecast error as a percentage of actual demand. Lower values indicate better forecast accuracy. Key KPI for demand planning performance.']
  mrp_area_code varchar [note: 'MRP area code defining the planning scope and organizational unit for material requirements planning execution.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this forecast record, capturing planner insights, assumptions, or special considerations.']
  outlier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forecast was flagged as a statistical outlier requiring review. Used for data quality monitoring and exception management.']
  outlier_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why this forecast was flagged as an outlier, documenting the business or statistical reason for the exception.']
  planning_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the planning group or demand planner responsible for this forecast. Used for workload distribution and accountability.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the planning period for which this forecast applies. Defines the end of the demand horizon.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the planning period for which this forecast applies. Defines the beginning of the demand horizon.']
  product_lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the product in its lifecycle. Influences forecasting model selection and demand pattern expectations.']
  promotional_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forecast includes promotional or campaign-driven demand uplift. Used to separate base demand from promotional spikes.']
  promotional_uplift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage increase in demand due to promotional activities during the planning period. Used for promotional planning and inventory pre-positioning.']
  sales_adjustment_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Manual adjustment quantity applied by sales team to the statistical baseline forecast, reflecting market intelligence and customer commitments.']
  sales_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for the sales adjustment, documenting market intelligence, customer commitments, or promotional activities driving the override.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Name of the planning scenario this forecast belongs to (e.g., Base Case, Optimistic, Pessimistic, New Product Launch). Enables scenario comparison and sensitivity analysis.']
  seasonality_index decimal(6,3) [note: 'Seasonal adjustment factor applied to the forecast for this planning period. Values above 1.0 indicate peak season, below 1.0 indicate off-season.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system that generated or provided this forecast record (e.g., D365_SCM, SAP_APO, CUSTOM_MODEL).']
  trend_coefficient decimal(10,6) [note: 'Trend component coefficient from the forecasting model, indicating the rate of demand growth or decline over time.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the forecast quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter). Must align with material master base UOM.']
  version_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast version: baseline (statistical model output), sales_adjusted (sales team input), consensus (S&OP aligned), approved (final for MRP), or working (draft).']

  Note: 'Stores version-aware demand forecast records supporting scenario comparison and consensus planning. Captures statistical baseline forecasts, sales-adjusted forecasts, consensus forecasts, and approved forecasts with full version lineage. Each record includes forecast quantity, forecast horizon, forecast model used, confidence interval, demand class (independent vs. dependent), item/location combination, version type, version approval workflow status, and planning period. Supports S&OP processes by maintaining multiple demand scenarios for supply planning sensitivity analysis. Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM demand planning module.'
}

Table supply.plan {
  plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for plan']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: S&OP Budget Alignment: Supply plans must reference the finance budget they operate under for budget vs. actual variance reporting in S&OP. Manufacturing finance controllers require this link to valida']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Long-term framework contracts in industrial manufacturing define committed supply volumes that directly drive supply plans. Planners reference the governing contract to enforce contractual delivery sc']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Supply Plan vs Budget variance analysis requires linking each supply plan to its responsible cost center.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Specific material or SKU for which this supply plan is created. May be null for aggregate material group plans.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Large strategic opportunities (e.g., major automation system projects) trigger dedicated supply plans in industrial manufacturing. Supply planners must trace which opportunity drove a plan for S&OP fe']
  plant_data_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plant_data. Business justification: Supply plans are created for a specific material-plant combination; plant_data holds the MRP parameters (lot_size_procedure, planned_delivery_time_days, procurement_type) the plan is based on. This FK']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: Supply plans are plant-specific in manufacturing. plan has plant_code as a plain denormalized attribute. A proper FK to production.plant enables plant-level supply plan filtering, multi-plant planning']
  product_revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_revision. Business justification: Supply plans in industrial manufacturing are revision-sensitive — an ECO-driven product revision may invalidate existing plans (BOM changes, process changes). Linking plan to product_revision enables ']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Supply plans reference the SKU master to allocate production capacity and material supply per product.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: Supply plans are created at plant/warehouse level in manufacturing. plant_code on plan is a denormalized plain-text reference to warehouse. This FK enables plant-level supply plan reporting, warehouse']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supply plan was approved, marking the transition from planning to execution phase.']
  capacity_utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of available production or procurement capacity utilized by this supply plan, indicating capacity constraints.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this supply plan record was first created in the system.']
  demand_forecast_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total forecasted demand quantity for the planning period, serving as the primary input to the supply plan.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this supply plan record was last updated, tracking the most recent change to planning parameters or status.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Total lead time in days from order placement to material availability, including procurement, production, and transportation time.']
  lot_sizing_procedure varchar [note: 'Lot-sizing rule used to determine planned order quantities: LOT_FOR_LOT (exact requirement), FIXED_LOT (fixed quantity), EOQ (Economic Order Quantity), POQ (Periodic Order Quantity), or PERIOD_LOT (period-based aggregation).']
  material_group_code varchar [note: 'Material group or product family covered by this supply plan, enabling aggregated planning at the product category level.']
  maximum_lot_size decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum order quantity constraint applied during supply planning, reflecting capacity or storage limitations.']
  minimum_lot_size decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum order quantity (MOQ) constraint applied during supply planning, reflecting supplier or production constraints.']
  mrp_controller_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the MRP controller or planner responsible for this supply plan, enabling workload distribution and accountability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments from planners regarding assumptions, constraints, or special considerations for this supply plan.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the supply plan, used for cross-system reference and S&OP review documentation.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supply plan: DRAFT (under development), PENDING_REVIEW (awaiting S&OP approval), APPROVED (approved for execution), ACTIVE (currently executing), SUPERSEDED (replaced by newer version), or CANCELLED (voided).']
  planned_supply_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of material planned to be supplied during the planning period, aggregated across all time buckets.']
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days into the future covered by this supply plan, defining the forward-looking planning window.']
  planning_method varchar [note: 'Method used to generate the supply plan: MRP (Material Requirements Planning), MRP II (Manufacturing Resource Planning), APS (Advanced Planning and Scheduling), MANUAL (manual planning), JIT (Just In Time), or KANBAN (pull-based replenishment).']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan.']
  planning_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the MRP/APS planning run was executed to generate this supply plan.']
  planning_strategy varchar [note: 'Supply planning strategy: MTS (Make to Stock), MTO (Make to Order), ATO (Assemble to Order), or ETO (Engineer to Order).']
  planning_time_fence_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from today within which the planning system will not automatically change planned orders, protecting near-term execution stability.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Source of supply: IN_HOUSE (internal production), EXTERNAL (external procurement from suppliers), or BOTH (mixed sourcing).']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Reorder point quantity that triggers replenishment planning when stock falls below this threshold.']
  rounding_value decimal(18,3) [note: 'Rounding increment for planned order quantities, ensuring orders align with packaging or production batch multiples.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Safety stock level maintained as buffer against demand variability and supply uncertainty, factored into the supply plan.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system that generated this supply plan: SAP_PP (SAP Production Planning), DYNAMICS_SCM (Microsoft Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management), OPCENTER (Siemens Opcenter APS), or MANUAL (manually created).']
  supply_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level for supply availability: LOW (stable supply), MEDIUM (minor risks), HIGH (significant risks), or CRITICAL (severe supply constraints).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the planned supply quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter, M for meter).']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Variance expressed as a percentage of demand forecast, enabling normalized comparison across materials.']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Variance between planned supply quantity and demand forecast quantity, indicating over-planning or under-planning.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the supply plan, enabling comparison of multiple planning scenarios and what-if analysis.']

  Note: 'Master supply plan record representing the approved supply response to demand for a given planning period, plant, and material group. Captures planned supply quantities by time bucket, planning method (MRP, APS, manual), plan version, approval status, planning parameters (strategy, horizon, time fences, lot-sizing procedure, MRP controller), and variance against demand forecast. Integrates outputs from SAP PP and Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM APS scheduling into a unified supply position for S&OP review.'
}

Table supply.capacity_plan {
  capacity_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the capacity plan record. Primary key for capacity planning analysis.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Capacity Budget Authorization: Capacity plans for overtime, tooling, and resource acquisition require authorization against a finance budget. Industrial manufacturing operations require this link for ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Capacity Cost Allocation process charges capacity usage to cost centers for internal cost tracking.']
  mrp_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.mrp_run. Business justification: A capacity plan is produced by a specific MRP run — the run triggers BOM explosion, routing explosion, and capacity load calculation. capacity_plan already carries last_mrp_run_date as a denormalized ']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: In industrial manufacturing, large opportunities (e.g., factory automation projects) trigger dedicated capacity plans to assess production feasibility before committing. Capacity planners need to link']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: A capacity plan is a sub-component of the master supply plan, recording work center and resource capacity data in support of the overall supply response. The plan entity is the parent; capacity_plan i']
  plant_data_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or facility where the work center is located. Enables multi-plant capacity planning.']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: Capacity plans use routing data to calculate required capacity hours per work center. The routing defines operation sequences and standard times that feed directly into capacity requirements planning.']
  vendor_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Capacity plans in industrial manufacturing include subcontracted and outsourced work center capacity. When a work center is operated by an external supplier, the capacity plan must reference the suppl']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or production resource for which capacity is being planned. Links to the production facility or machine group.']
  available_capacity_hours decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total available capacity in hours for the work center during the planning period. Calculated based on shifts, calendar days, and resource availability.']
  capacity_buffer_hours decimal(18,2) [note: 'Safety buffer or protective capacity reserved to absorb variability and prevent schedule disruptions. Part of Theory of Constraints buffer management.']
  capacity_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the capacity resource based on criticality to production flow. Critical resources are closely monitored for bottlenecks.']
  capacity_load_profile varchar [note: 'Pattern of capacity demand over the planning period. Indicates whether load is evenly distributed, concentrated in peaks, or highly variable.']
  capacity_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that generated the capacity plan data. Typically SAP PP, Siemens Opcenter APS, or Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for capacity planning. Typically hours for time-based capacity or units/pieces for output-based capacity.']
  capacity_utilization_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of available capacity that is utilized by required capacity. Calculated as (required_capacity_hours / available_capacity_hours) * 100. Key metric for identifying overload or underutilization.']
  critical_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of time remaining until due date to work time remaining. Used for priority scheduling and capacity allocation. Values less than 1.0 indicate behind schedule.']
  efficiency_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Efficiency factor applied to available capacity to account for actual performance versus standard. Expressed as percentage. Used to adjust theoretical capacity to realistic capacity.']
  is_bottleneck boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this work center is identified as a bottleneck constraint in the production schedule. True if utilization exceeds threshold or if this resource limits throughput.']
  leveling_adjustment_hours decimal(18,2) [note: 'Capacity adjustment applied through leveling actions such as overtime, shift changes, subcontracting, or order rescheduling to balance load.']
  leveling_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy applied to resolve capacity overload. Indicates the method used to balance capacity constraints.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'MRP controller code responsible for the materials and capacity planning for this work center. Links capacity planning to material planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments from the planner regarding capacity constraints, assumptions, or leveling decisions.']
  overload_hours decimal(18,2) [note: 'Number of hours by which required capacity exceeds available capacity. Positive value indicates capacity shortage requiring leveling or additional resources.']
  plan_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capacity plan record was created in the system.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capacity plan. Indicates whether the plan is in draft, approved for execution, actively used, or archived.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Type of capacity planning method applied. RCCP (Rough-Cut Capacity Planning) for aggregate planning, CRP (Capacity Requirements Planning) for detailed planning, infinite for unconstrained, finite for constrained scheduling.']
  plan_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capacity plan record was last updated or recalculated.']
  planned_downtime_hours decimal(18,2) [note: 'Hours of planned maintenance, changeovers, or scheduled downtime that reduce available capacity during the planning period.']
  planning_horizon varchar [note: 'Time granularity of the capacity plan. Indicates whether this is a short-term, medium-term, or long-term capacity plan.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the capacity planning period. Defines the end of the time bucket for capacity analysis.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the capacity planning period. Defines the beginning of the time bucket for capacity analysis.']
  planning_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the capacity plan. Supports scenario planning and what-if analysis by maintaining multiple plan versions.']
  queue_time_hours decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average queue time or wait time for jobs at this work center during the planning period. Indicates congestion and scheduling delays.']
  required_capacity_hours decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total required capacity in hours derived from planned orders, production orders, and demand forecasts for the planning period.']
  resource_type varchar [note: 'Type of production resource being planned. Distinguishes between machine capacity, labor capacity, tooling, or other equipment.']
  run_time_hours decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total productive run time or processing time required for planned orders during the planning period.']
  setup_time_hours decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total setup or changeover time required during the planning period. Reduces available productive capacity.']
  shift_count varchar [note: 'Number of production shifts scheduled for the work center during the planning period. Used to calculate available capacity.']
  shift_hours_per_day decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total productive hours per day across all shifts. Excludes breaks and non-productive time.']
  underload_hours decimal(18,2) [note: 'Number of hours by which available capacity exceeds required capacity. Positive value indicates spare capacity available for additional orders or maintenance.']
  unplanned_downtime_hours decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or historical hours of unplanned downtime (breakdowns, failures) factored into capacity planning to provide realistic capacity estimates.']

  Note: 'Records capacity planning data for work centers and production resources, including available capacity, required capacity derived from planned orders, utilization rate, bottleneck flags, and capacity leveling adjustments. Supports rough-cut capacity planning (RCCP) and detailed capacity planning (CRP) as executed in SAP PP and Siemens Opcenter APS. Enables identification of capacity constraints before production scheduling.'
}

  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered from supplier or produced in a single production run.']

  mrp_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the MRP run that generated this requirement record was executed.']

  pegging_requirement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent requirement that triggered this dependent requirement through BOM explosion (used for requirement traceability).']
  planned_delivery_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected lead time in days from order placement to material receipt, used for backward scheduling.']
  planned_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The lot-size adjusted quantity that MRP proposes to procure or produce, after applying lot-sizing rules (MOQ, rounding, fixed lot size).']
  planning_time_fence_days varchar [note: 'Number of days into the future during which MRP will not automatically create or change planned orders without planner approval.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates how the material is procured: E (in-house production), F (external procurement), X (both).']
  projected_available_balance decimal(18,3) [note: 'Projected on-hand inventory balance after accounting for gross requirements and scheduled receipts up to this requirement date.']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered to avoid stockout.']
  requirement_date date [note: 'The date on which the material is needed to meet demand or production schedule.']
  requirement_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking used to sequence requirements when allocating limited supply or production capacity (lower number = higher priority).']
  requirement_source_item varchar [note: 'The line item number within the source document that triggered this requirement.']
  requirement_source_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the source that triggered this requirement (e.g., sales order number, forecast ID, planned order number).']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment status of the material requirement: open (not yet covered), partially covered (some supply allocated), fully covered (sufficient supply allocated), cancelled, or expired.']
  rounding_value decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity increment to which planned orders are rounded up (e.g., round to nearest pallet quantity or container size).']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum inventory level maintained as buffer against demand variability and supply uncertainty.']
  scheduled_receipt_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material expected to be received from open purchase orders, production orders, or transfer orders by the requirement date.']
  special_procurement_type varchar [note: 'Code indicating special procurement scenarios such as subcontracting, consignment, stock transfer, or third-party order processing.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which material quantities are expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter).']

  Note: 'Individual material requirement record produced by MRP, representing the net requirement for a specific material at a specific plant and date. Captures gross requirement, scheduled receipts, projected available balance, net requirement, lot-size adjusted requirement, and the requirement source (sales order, forecast, safety stock). Core output of SAP PP MRP runs used to drive procurement and production planning.'
}

  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Specific location or port associated with the Incoterms designation, defining the point of delivery or risk transfer.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this sourcing rule record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sourcing rule record was last updated or modified.']

  lot_size_rounding_value decimal(18,3) [note: 'Rounding increment for order quantities. MRP will round calculated requirements up to the nearest multiple of this value to align with packaging or production batch constraints.']
  lot_sizing_procedure varchar [note: 'SAP lot-sizing procedure code defining how MRP calculates order quantities: EX (lot-for-lot), HB (fixed lot size), FB (replenish to maximum), PD (period lot size), WM (weekly lot size), MB (monthly lot size).']
  make_or_buy_indicator varchar [note: 'Strategic decision indicator specifying whether the material is manufactured in-house (make), procured externally (buy), or both options are available.']
  maximum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum quantity that can be ordered in a single purchase requisition or production order under this sourcing rule, based on supplier capacity or production constraints.']
  moq decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity negotiated with the supplier or defined by production constraints. MRP will not generate orders below this threshold.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments providing context, special instructions, or historical information about this sourcing rule.']
  order_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure in which orders are placed with the supplier (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Must be convertible to base UOM.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Code representing the negotiated payment terms with the supplier, defining due dates, discounts, and payment schedules.']
  planned_delivery_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected lead time in calendar days from order placement to goods receipt for this material-plant-supplier combination. Used by MRP for backward scheduling.']
  planner_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sourcing rule has been reviewed and approved by the material planner or supply chain manager for use in MRP runs.']
  preferred_supplier_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator marking this supplier as the preferred source for the material-plant combination. Used to prioritize sourcing decisions when multiple suppliers are available.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'Quantity unit to which the standard price applies. For example, if price_unit is 100, the standard_price represents the cost per 100 units.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Code identifying the purchasing group or buyer responsible for managing procurement activities under this sourcing rule.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Code identifying the purchasing organization responsible for negotiating and executing procurement under this sourcing rule.']
  quota_arrangement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sourcing rule is part of a quota arrangement where demand is split across multiple suppliers based on allocation percentages.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code identifying this sourcing rule for operational reference and reporting.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the sourcing rule for business user identification and reporting purposes.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sourcing rule indicating whether it is active and available for MRP, temporarily blocked, awaiting approval, or expired.']
  sourcing_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking indicating the preference order when multiple sourcing rules exist for the same material-plant combination. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  sourcing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sourcing method: external procurement from supplier, in-house production, subcontracting, stock transfer between plants, or consignment arrangement.']
  standard_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard negotiated unit price for the material from this supplier, used for cost estimation and procurement planning. Excludes taxes and freight.']
  supply_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of supply chain risk associated with this sourcing rule, considering supplier reliability, geopolitical factors, lead time variability, and single-source dependency.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date from which this sourcing rule becomes effective and can be used by MRP for procurement planning.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'End date after which this sourcing rule is no longer valid and will not be considered by MRP. Null indicates indefinite validity.']

  Note: 'Defines the complete sourcing logic, lot-sizing constraints, and MOQ parameters for each material-plant-supplier combination. Specifies preferred suppliers, make-vs-buy decisions, split sourcing percentages, sourcing priority, applicable date ranges, Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ), maximum order quantity, lot size rounding values, fixed lot sizes, order quantity constraints, MOQ negotiation history, and planner-approved exceptions. These rules directly govern how MRP generates planned purchase requisitions or production orders and enforce procurement cost optimization through lot-size calculations. Managed in SAP MM and Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM.'
}

  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Safety stock policies in industrial manufacturing are defined at the storage location level. storage_location_code on supply_safety_stock_policy is a denormalized plain-text reference to stock_locatio']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Safety‑stock policies are often defined per supplier to reflect reliability and lead‑time variability, required for accurate buffer calculations.']
  vendor_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Safety stock policies are site-specific: a supplier site\'s lead time variability and capacity directly drive safety stock calculations. Planners need to reference the specific supplier site to apply c']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: Safety stock policies are plant-scoped in industrial manufacturing. plant_code on supply_safety_stock_policy is a denormalized plain-text reference to warehouse. This FK enables plant-level safety sto']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'Inventory classification based on value and criticality: A (high-value, tight control), B (moderate-value), C (low-value, loose control).']
  average_daily_demand decimal(18,3) [note: 'Mean daily consumption or usage rate of the material, used as input for reorder point and safety stock calculations.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to determine safety stock levels: static (fixed quantity), dynamic (demand-driven), statistical (based on demand variability and service level), or manual (user-defined).']
  coverage_profile varchar [note: 'Six-character code referencing a time-phased coverage profile used in advanced planning systems for dynamic safety stock calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this safety stock policy record was first created in the system.']
  criticality_code varchar [note: 'Business criticality rating indicating the impact of stockout on operations: critical (production stoppage), high (significant disruption), medium (moderate impact), low (minimal impact).']
  demand_variability_coefficient decimal(8,4) [note: 'Statistical measure of demand fluctuation (coefficient of variation or standard deviation) used in statistical safety stock calculations.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this safety stock policy becomes active and is used in MRP calculations.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date on which this safety stock policy expires or is superseded by a new policy. Null indicates an open-ended policy.']
  holding_cost_percent_annual decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual inventory carrying cost as a percentage of material value, including warehousing, insurance, obsolescence, and cost of capital.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this safety stock policy record was most recently updated.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which this safety stock policy was reviewed and validated by supply chain planning.']
  lead_time_variability_days decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard deviation of supplier lead time in days, used to account for supply uncertainty in safety stock calculations.']
  lot_size_rule varchar [note: 'SAP lot-sizing procedure code: EX (Lot-for-Lot), HB (Replenish to Maximum), FX (Fixed Lot Size), WB (Weekly Lot Size), MB (Monthly Lot Size).']
  maximum_stock_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'The upper inventory limit for this material-plant combination, used to prevent overstocking and optimize working capital.']
  minimum_stock_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'The lower inventory threshold below which stock should not fall under normal operating conditions.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this safety stock policy record.']
  moq_minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The smallest order quantity that can be procured from the supplier, constraining replenishment order sizes.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'Three-character code identifying the MRP controller responsible for exception management and planning parameter maintenance.']
  mrp_type varchar [note: 'MRP procedure type that determines how material requirements are planned: PD (MRP), VB (Manual Reorder Point), VM (Forecast-Based Planning), VV (Time-Phased Planning), R1 (Reorder Point Planning), R2 (Replenishment Planning).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next review of this safety stock policy.']
  planned_delivery_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of calendar days from order placement to material receipt, used in reorder point calculation.']
  planner_code varchar [note: 'Three-character code identifying the supply chain planner or buyer responsible for managing this material-plant combination.']
  policy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for planners to document special considerations, assumptions, or business rules governing this safety stock policy.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the safety stock policy: active (in use), inactive (not applied), suspended (temporarily disabled), under_review (pending approval).']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'SAP procurement type indicator: E (In-house production), F (External procurement), X (Both).']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered, calculated as safety stock plus demand during lead time.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled reviews of this safety stock policy to ensure continued relevance and accuracy.']
  rounding_value decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity increment to which replenishment orders are rounded (e.g., round to nearest pallet quantity).']
  safety_days_supply varchar [note: 'Number of days of average demand that the safety stock quantity is intended to cover.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The buffer stock quantity maintained to protect against demand variability and supply uncertainty, expressed in base unit of measure.']
  safety_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days added to the planned delivery lead time as a time buffer to account for supply uncertainty.']
  service_level_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target probability of not experiencing a stockout during the lead time, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00 for 95% service level).']
  special_procurement_key varchar [note: 'Two-digit SAP code indicating special procurement scenarios such as subcontracting, consignment, stock transfer, or third-party processing.']
  stockout_cost_per_unit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost impact per unit of stockout, including lost sales, expediting costs, and production disruption, used in economic safety stock optimization.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure in which safety stock quantities are expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter).']
  xyz_classification varchar [note: 'Demand variability classification: X (stable demand), Y (moderate variability), Z (highly variable or sporadic demand).']

  Note: 'Defines safety stock parameters and reorder point policies for each material-plant combination, including safety stock quantity, safety days of supply, service level target, reorder point, maximum stock level, and the calculation method (static, dynamic, statistical). Captures the business rules governing buffer stock levels to protect against demand variability and supplier lead time uncertainty. Managed in SAP MM material master planning data.'
}

Table supply.demand_plan_version {
  demand_plan_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand plan version record. Primary key for the demand plan version entity.']
  demand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent demand plan that this version belongs to. Links to the master demand plan entity.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Demand plan versions in industrial manufacturing S&OP are created and managed at the product family level (family-level statistical forecasting is standard). This FK enables S&OP teams to aggregate de']
  superseded_by_version_demand_plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer demand plan version that replaces this version. Null if this is the current active version. Maintains version lineage and audit trail for plan evolution tracking.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: Demand plan versions are plant-scoped in industrial manufacturing. plant_code on demand_plan_version is a denormalized plain-text reference to warehouse. This FK enables plant-level demand planning ve']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this demand plan version was formally approved by authorized stakeholders. Null if version has not yet been approved. Marks the transition to executable plan status.']
  approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this demand plan version has received formal approval through the S&OP governance process. True if approved for execution, false if still in draft or review.']
  baseline_version_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this version serves as the baseline reference for variance analysis and scenario comparison. True if this is the authoritative baseline, false for alternative scenarios or working versions.']
  bias_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Systematic tendency of this demand plan version to over-forecast (positive bias) or under-forecast (negative bias) actual demand. Calculated as the sum of forecast errors divided by sum of actuals, used to detect and correct persistent forecasting errors.']
  confidence_level_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level associated with the demand forecast in this version. Represents the probability that actual demand will fall within the forecasted range, typically 80%, 90%, or 95% confidence intervals used for safety stock calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this demand plan version record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking.']
  demand_time_fence_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from the current date within which demand changes are frozen or restricted. Defines the period where MRP (Material Requirements Planning) will not automatically adjust planned orders based on forecast changes, protecting near-term production stability.']
  forecast_accuracy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured accuracy of this demand plan version compared to actual demand realization. Calculated as 100 minus the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), used for continuous improvement of forecasting methods and model selection.']
  forecast_model_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the statistical or algorithmic model used to generate demand forecasts in this version (e.g., ARIMA, exponential smoothing, machine learning model). Applicable when planning method is statistical or APS.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this demand plan version record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization across systems.']
  material_group_code varchar [note: 'Product family or material group classification for demand aggregation. Used to organize demand planning at the product category level before disaggregation to individual SKUs (Stock Keeping Units).']
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days covered by this demand plan version. Calculated as the difference between planning horizon end and start dates, used for capacity planning and lead time analysis.']
  planning_horizon_end_date date [note: 'Last date of the planning period covered by this demand plan version. Defines the end of the forecast window, typically aligned with fiscal quarters or annual planning cycles.']
  planning_horizon_start_date date [note: 'First date of the planning period covered by this demand plan version. Defines the beginning of the forecast window for supply planning and MRP (Material Requirements Planning) execution.']
  planning_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to generate this demand plan version. Statistical uses algorithmic forecasting, collaborative incorporates cross-functional input, consensus reflects S&OP agreement, APS (Advanced Planning and Scheduling) uses optimization engines, and manual indicates planner-driven creation.']
  planning_time_fence_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from the current date within which supply planning changes require manual approval. Defines the period where automated MRP adjustments are restricted to prevent disruption to committed production schedules.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Business name for the planning scenario represented by this version. Used for what-if analysis and sensitivity testing in S&OP processes (e.g., Optimistic Growth, Conservative Base, Supply Constrained).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the operational system that created or owns this demand plan version record. Typically Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM, SAP APO (Advanced Planning and Optimization), or specialized demand planning applications for data lineage and integration traceability.']
  total_planned_demand_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate demand quantity across all materials and time buckets within this plan version. Represents the total forecasted demand volume for supply network capacity planning and scenario comparison.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the total planned demand quantity. Typically base inventory unit (EA, KG, L, M) aligned with material master UOM definitions in SAP MM or Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM.']
  version_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text commentary and business context for this demand plan version. Captures assumptions, market conditions, promotional events, supply constraints, or other factors influencing the forecast that are relevant for scenario interpretation and decision-making.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the demand plan version. Human-readable version code used for tracking and referencing specific plan iterations (e.g., V1.0, V2.1, BASELINE-2024Q1).']
  version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demand plan version. Tracks the approval workflow state from initial draft through review, approval, and eventual archival or supersession by newer versions.']
  version_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the demand plan version indicating its purpose and stage in the S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning) process. Baseline represents initial statistical forecast, sales-adjusted includes sales team input, consensus reflects cross-functional agreement, approved is the final authorized plan, and working is an in-progress draft.']
  creation_date date [note: 'Date when this demand plan version was created. Represents the business event timestamp for version instantiation in the S&OP cycle.']

  Note: 'Manages versioned demand plan records enabling scenario comparison and consensus planning. Captures plan version identifier, version type (baseline statistical, sales-adjusted, consensus, approved), creation date, planning horizon, total planned demand quantity, and version approval workflow status. Supports S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning) processes by maintaining multiple demand scenarios for supply planning sensitivity analysis in Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM.'
}

Table customer.account {
  account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer account record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the customer_account master data product. Serves as the SSOT reference key for all downstream domains including sales, service, order, and billing.']
  business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.business_partner. Business justification: In SAP-based industrial manufacturing ERP, every customer account maps to a business partner master record for unified MDM, payment terms, and compliance management. customer_account.sap_business_part']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Legal‑entity invoicing and tax reporting assign each customer to a company code for statutory compliance.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost Allocation Report requires mapping each customer account to a cost center for internal expense tracking per strategic account.']
  parent_account_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the parent customer_account record in the corporate hierarchy. Enables multi-level account hierarchy modeling (e.g., subsidiary → division → global parent). Null for top-level accounts.']
  price_book_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SAP S/4HANA pricing condition group or price list assigned to this customer. Determines applicable pricing, discounts, and surcharges during sales order creation.']
  primary_sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Primary sales rep on an account drives quota allocation, forecasting, and territory performance metrics in industrial manufacturing.']
  primary_ultimate_parent_account_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the topmost entity in the corporate hierarchy for this customer account. Supports global account consolidation, enterprise-level revenue reporting, and strategic account management.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis per customer uses profit_center_id on the account to attribute revenue and margin to the correct profit center.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Direct FK to sales territory replaces the denormalized code, supporting territory‑based reporting and incentive calculations.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Linking account to its segment enables normalization of segment data and removes redundant string field.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Procurement planning and supplier performance dashboards require each account to designate a primary supplier.']
  account_source varchar [note: 'Channel or method through which this customer account was originally acquired. Used for marketing attribution, channel effectiveness analysis, and sales pipeline reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct_sales|referral|trade_show|inbound_marketing|partner|acquisition|existing_relationship — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the customer account. Controls whether transactions, orders, and service requests can be processed against this account. Managed in Salesforce CRM and synchronized to SAP S/4HANA.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the customer\'s commercial role in the industrial supply chain. Drives pricing tiers, contract templates, and service entitlements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OEM|distributor|system_integrator|end_user|EPC_contractor|reseller|value_added_reseller|direct — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  annual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Customer organization\'s most recently reported annual revenue in the account\'s local currency. Used for customer segmentation, credit risk assessment, and strategic account classification. Currency indicated by revenue_currency_code.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City of the customer\'s primary billing address. Used for geographic segmentation, logistics planning, and tax jurisdiction determination.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the customer\'s primary billing address country. Used for tax jurisdiction determination, regulatory compliance, and geographic segmentation.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the customer\'s primary billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction mapping, logistics routing, and geographic analytics.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the customer\'s primary billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction determination, sales territory assignment, and regional reporting.']
  close_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer account was formally closed or terminated. Null for active accounts. Used for churn analysis, lifecycle reporting, and regulatory record retention.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer account record was first created in the source system of record (Salesforce CRM). Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding accounts receivable balance permitted for this customer account, denominated in the account\'s billing currency. Enforced in SAP S/4HANA SD order processing and credit management workflows.']
  credit_limit_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the credit_limit field. Ensures correct interpretation of the credit limit in multi-currency environments.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating assigned to the customer by an internal credit team or external agency (e.g., Dun & Bradstreet, Moody\'s). Used to determine credit limits, payment terms, and order approval workflows in SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  crm_account_code varchar [note: 'Native Account record ID from Salesforce CRM (Account object). Used to cross-reference the master record back to the operational source system for reconciliation and lineage tracing.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the completeness and accuracy of the customer account record as assessed by Informatica MDM data quality rules. Used to prioritize data stewardship activities and MDM enrichment workflows.']
  distribution_channel_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA distribution channel code indicating how products are sold to this customer (e.g., direct sales, wholesale, OEM channel). Part of the SAP SD organizational assignment.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet DUNS number uniquely identifying the customer\'s legal entity globally. Used for credit risk assessment, supplier/customer verification, and corporate hierarchy resolution.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'Total number of employees at the customer organization as reported or estimated. Used for customer segmentation (SMB vs. enterprise), credit risk scoring, and market sizing analytics.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'ICC Incoterms code defining the agreed delivery and risk transfer conditions for shipments to this customer. Used in SAP S/4HANA SD sales order processing and logistics planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  industry_naics_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit NAICS code identifying the customer\'s primary industry sector per North American classification standards. Complements SIC code for more granular industry segmentation and analytics.']
  industry_sic_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit Standard Industrial Classification code identifying the customer\'s primary industry sector. Used for market segmentation, regulatory reporting, and credit risk assessment.']
  is_global_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer operates across multiple countries and is managed under a global account management program with consolidated commercial terms and cross-regional coordination.']
  is_strategic_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer is designated as a strategic account requiring executive-level relationship management and customized service agreements. Strategic accounts receive dedicated account teams and priority support.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent commercial activity (order, service request, or interaction) recorded against this customer account. Used for account health monitoring, dormancy detection, and re-engagement campaigns.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the customer account record in the source system of record. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the B2B customer organization as recorded in official corporate filings. Used for invoicing, contracts, and regulatory compliance. Must match the name on file with the relevant tax authority.']
  mdm_golden_record_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record has been designated as the MDM golden (master) record for the customer entity after deduplication and survivorship processing in Informatica MDM. False indicates a candidate or survivor record pending promotion.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer account was formally opened and activated in the system of record. Marks the start of the commercial relationship. Used for customer tenure calculations and cohort analysis.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary business telephone number for the customer organization. Used for account management communications and service escalation. Stored in E.164-compatible format.']
  preferred_language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 region suffix) indicating the customer\'s preferred language for communications, documents, and correspondence (e.g., en-US, de-DE, fr-FR).']
  revenue_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the annual_revenue field (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required to correctly interpret the revenue figure for multi-currency global accounts.']
  sales_organization_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA sales organization code responsible for selling to this customer. Determines the legal entity, currency, and pricing procedures applicable to sales transactions.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'SLA tier assigned to this customer account, defining the service response times, support priority, and escalation paths applicable to service requests and after-sales support. Managed in Salesforce Service Cloud.']
  status_date date [note: 'Date on which the current account_status became effective. Supports lifecycle history tracking and audit compliance. Used to calculate duration in current status for account health reporting.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the customer\'s legal entity (e.g., EIN in the US, CRN in the UK). Required for invoicing, tax reporting, and regulatory compliance under IFRS/GAAP.']
  trading_name varchar [note: 'Commercial or brand name under which the customer operates if different from the legal entity name. Used in sales communications, account management, and CRM displays.']
  vat_registration_number varchar [note: 'VAT registration number for the customer entity, required for EU and international tax compliance. Used in invoice generation and cross-border transaction reporting.']
  website varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the customer organization. Used for account research, digital engagement tracking, and customer profile enrichment.']

  Note: 'Master record for all B2B industrial customers, OEM accounts, distributors, and end-user organizations. Serves as the SSOT for customer identity in Salesforce CRM (Account object) and SAP Business Partner. Captures legal entity name, industry classification (SIC/NAICS), account type (OEM, distributor, system integrator, end-user, EPC contractor), account status with full lifecycle history, annual revenue, employee count, DUNS number, tax ID, VAT registration, credit rating, assigned account manager, parent account reference, and strategic account flag. Foundation entity referenced by all other products in this domain and cross-referenced by sales, service, order, and billing domains. Corporate hierarchy and commercial relationships are managed in account_relationship.'
}

Table customer.customer_contact {
  customer_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the contact record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent B2B customer account (OEM, distributor, or end-user organization) to which this contact belongs. Establishes the person-to-account relationship.']
  reports_to_contact_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the contact\'s organizational superior within the same account. Enables modeling of the customer-side org chart for multi-stakeholder enterprise sales and service engagement strategies.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Assigning a sales rep to each contact enables contact‑level sales follow‑up and pipeline reporting, a standard practice in manufacturing account management.']
  account_site varchar [note: 'Specific plant, facility, or site within the parent account organization where this contact is based. Critical for industrial manufacturing customers with multiple production sites, enabling site-level service and sales targeting.']
  assistant_name varchar [note: 'Name of the contact\'s executive assistant or administrative coordinator. Used to facilitate scheduling, correspondence routing, and executive-level engagement for senior stakeholders at key accounts.']
  assistant_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the contact\'s executive assistant. Enables sales and service teams to reach senior contacts through their administrative support channel.']
  birthdate date [note: 'Date of birth of the contact. Captured selectively for key account contacts where relationship management programs require it. Subject to strict GDPR data minimization principles — only collected when there is a documented lawful basis.']
  consent_record_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to the formal consent record in the consent management system. Provides an auditable trail of the lawful basis under which this contact\'s personal data is processed, as required by GDPR Article 7.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact\'s decision-making role in the B2B buying and service process. Drives sales engagement strategy and marketing segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: technical_evaluator|commercial_buyer|executive_approver|end_user|influencer|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contact record was first created in the source system (Salesforce CRM). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED anchor for data lineage, GDPR data retention calculations, and contact age analytics.']
  crm_contact_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this contact record as assigned by Salesforce CRM. Used for cross-system reconciliation and delta-load processing from the operational source of record.']
  customer_contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact record. Governs whether the contact is eligible for outreach, service engagement, and data processing activities. \'do_not_contact\' enforces opt-out and regulatory suppression requirements.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the completeness and accuracy of this contact record as assessed by Informatica MDM data quality rules. Drives data stewardship prioritization and master data governance workflows.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or functional unit to which the contact belongs at their employer (e.g., Engineering, Procurement, Manufacturing, Maintenance, Finance, Executive). Supports segmentation for targeted outreach.']
  do_not_call boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has requested to be excluded from telephone outreach. True = phone contact suppressed. Enforced in compliance with national do-not-call registries and GDPR consent requirements.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address of the contact. Used as the principal digital communication channel for sales outreach, service notifications, and marketing campaigns. Governed under GDPR and applicable privacy regulations.']
  email_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has explicitly opted out of email communications. True = email suppressed; False = email permitted. Distinct from marketing_opt_in as it applies specifically to all email types including transactional. Enforced by Salesforce CRM email suppression lists.']
  fax varchar [note: 'Fax number associated with the contact or their office location. Retained for legacy B2B industrial communication workflows and regulatory document exchange in certain jurisdictions.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the individual contact as recorded in Salesforce CRM. Used for personalized communication across sales, service, and marketing channels.']
  gdpr_data_subject_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to this contact as a GDPR data subject. Used to process data subject rights requests (access, erasure, portability) and maintain the record of processing activities (ROPA) as required under GDPR Article 30.']
  is_decision_maker boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact holds formal decision-making authority for purchasing or contracting at their organization. True = authorized decision maker. Used to prioritize sales engagement and opportunity progression in the CRM pipeline.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated as the primary point of contact for their account. True = primary contact for account-level communications and escalations. Only one contact per account should hold this designation.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title of the contact at their organization (e.g., Plant Manager, Procurement Manager, Design Engineer, Maintenance Supervisor, VP of Operations). Critical for routing sales and service engagements to the appropriate stakeholder.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code representing the contact\'s preferred language for communications and documentation (e.g., en, de, zh, fr, ja). Drives localization of marketing materials, service documents, and product manuals.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent logged activity (call, email, meeting, task) associated with this contact in Salesforce CRM. Used to identify dormant contacts, trigger re-engagement workflows, and measure sales cadence effectiveness.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this contact record in the source system (Salesforce CRM). Used for incremental data extraction, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail maintenance in the Silver Layer.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the individual contact. Combined with first_name to form the full display name used in correspondence and CRM records.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Channel or origin through which this contact was first acquired or identified. Used for marketing attribution, ROI analysis of demand generation programs, and sales pipeline reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: trade_show|web_inquiry|referral|partner|direct_sales|marketing_campaign|distributor|other — promote to reference product]']
  linkedin_url varchar [note: 'URL of the contact\'s LinkedIn professional profile. Used by sales and marketing teams for social selling, account-based marketing (ABM), and contact enrichment workflows.']
  mailing_city varchar [note: 'City component of the contact\'s mailing address. Used in geographic segmentation, territory assignment, and regional sales reporting.']
  mailing_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the contact\'s mailing address (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN). Drives multi-national compliance, language preference defaults, and export control screening.']
  mailing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code component of the contact\'s mailing address. Used for geographic routing, logistics planning, and regional analytics.']
  mailing_state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the contact\'s mailing address. Supports territory management, tax jurisdiction determination, and regional compliance reporting.']
  mailing_street varchar [note: 'Street address line for the contact\'s mailing address. Used for physical correspondence, contract delivery, and regulatory document dispatch.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has provided explicit consent to receive marketing communications. True = opted in; False = opted out or consent not obtained. Mandatory for GDPR and CAN-SPAM compliance. Must be False by default until affirmative consent is recorded.']
