Business justification: Procurement contracts (release orders/scheduling agreements in finance) are executed under overarching supplier framework agreements. This hierarchy is fundamental to contract management in industrial']
  commodity_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.commodity_category. Business justification: Procurement contracts contain a `material_category` STRING field that maps to the commodity category taxonomy. Replacing this with commodity_category_id FK normalizes contract classification to the au']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Procurement contracts are established under a specific company code (legal entity) in SAP MM. This FK links contracts to the legal entity master, enabling entity-level contract spend reporting, compli']
  product_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Supports \'Family‑Level Supply Contract Management\' allowing contracts to be associated with product families for volume‑discount negotiations.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor party with whom this procurement contract is established.']
  vendor_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.site. Business justification: Procurement contracts in industrial manufacturing are often site-specific — delivery terms, lead times, and MOQs are negotiated per supplier site. delivery_location is a plain-text denormalization of ']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of amendments or change orders issued against this contract since its original approval.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was formally approved by authorized signatories and became legally binding.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews upon expiration if not explicitly terminated. True for auto-renewing contracts, False otherwise.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the contract with internal procurement policies, regulatory requirements, and governance standards. Compliant indicates full adherence, non-compliant flags violations, under review indicates active audit, waived indicates approved exception.']
  confidentiality_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes confidentiality or non-disclosure provisions protecting proprietary information. True if confidentiality terms are present, False otherwise.']
  contract_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the scope, purpose, and key terms of the procurement contract.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the procurement contract for easy identification and reference.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the procurement contract, typically assigned by SAP Ariba or ERP system.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the procurement contract. Draft indicates initial creation, pending approval awaits authorization, active is in force, suspended is temporarily halted, expired has passed end date, terminated is cancelled before expiration, closed is completed and archived. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|expired|terminated|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the procurement contract structure. Blanket PO for recurring purchases with predefined terms, scheduling agreement for delivery schedules, value contract for spend-based limits, quantity contract for volume-based commitments, framework agreement for multi-supplier arrangements, or master agreement for overarching terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this procurement contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this contract (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the procurement contract becomes legally binding and operational.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the procurement contract term ends and is no longer in force. Nullable for open-ended contracts.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Standardized trade terms defining responsibilities for shipping, insurance, and risk transfer between buyer and supplier per ICC Incoterms 2020. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment or modification to the contract terms. Nullable if no amendments have been made.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this procurement contract record was last updated or modified.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard procurement lead time in days from purchase order release to delivery, as committed by the supplier in the contract.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity per release or purchase order required by the supplier under this contract. Nullable if no MOQ is specified.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated in the contract (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30 for early payment discounts).']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Description of financial penalties or liquidated damages applicable for supplier non-compliance with contract terms, SLA breaches, or quality failures.']
  price_deescalation_mechanism varchar [note: 'Formula or methodology for reducing contract prices based on volume commitments, market conditions, or continuous improvement targets.']
  price_escalation_mechanism varchar [note: 'Formula or methodology for adjusting contract prices over time based on indices (e.g., CPI, commodity indices), exchange rates, or negotiated percentage increases.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Buyer group or category team within the purchasing organization responsible for this contract, typically aligned to commodity or material category.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit or division responsible for negotiating and executing this procurement contract within the ERP system.']
  quality_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific quality standards, certifications, inspection criteria, and acceptance testing requirements mandated in the contract (e.g., ISO 9001, PPAP, APQP, specific Cpk targets).']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for target quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, M for meters, HR for hours).']
  release_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Cumulative quantity already released or called off against this contract through purchase orders or delivery schedules.']
  release_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value already released or spent against this contract through purchase orders.']
  remaining_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity still available for release under this contract, calculated as target quantity minus release quantity.']
  remaining_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value still available for release under this contract, calculated as total contract value minus release value.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months for each automatic renewal period if auto renewal is enabled. Nullable if auto renewal is not applicable.']
  sla_terms varchar [note: 'Service level commitments and performance targets defined in the contract, such as lead time, on-time delivery rate, quality standards, and response times.']
  target_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Planned or committed quantity of materials or services to be procured under this contract for quantity-based agreements. Nullable for value-based contracts.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the contract before expiration.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value committed under this procurement contract for value-based contracts, or estimated total spend for quantity-based contracts.']
  warranty_terms varchar [note: 'Warranty coverage and duration provided by the supplier for materials or services under this contract, including defect remediation and replacement terms.']

  Note: 'Master procurement contract or framework agreement established with a supplier for recurring supply of materials or services over a defined term. Captures contract number, contract type (blanket PO, scheduling agreement, value contract, quantity contract), effective/expiration dates, total contract value, target/release quantities, auto-renewal flag, SLA terms, penalty clauses, price escalation/de-escalation mechanisms, compliance status, and contract owner. Distinct from sales contracts owned by the sales domain. Supports contract coverage KPI tracking and maverick spend identification.'
}

Table finance.supplier_invoice {
  supplier_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier invoice record. Primary key.']
  commodity_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.commodity_category. Business justification: Supplier invoices contain a `material_category` STRING field that maps to the commodity category taxonomy. Replacing this with commodity_category_id FK normalizes spend classification to the authorita']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.company_code']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Invoice posting requires cost center to allocate vendor expenses to the correct internal unit.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Vendor invoices are posted to GL accounts for expense recognition per accounting policy.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'FK to production.plant']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.procurement_contract. Business justification: Supplier invoices are frequently issued against procurement contracts (blanket/framework agreements) in industrial manufacturing. Linking supplier_invoice to procurement_contract enables contract util']
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the goods receipt document used in three-way match verification.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order against which this invoice is matched in the three-way match process (PO–GR–Invoice).']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: supplier_invoice has material_number as a plain denormalized text field. Replacing with FK to sku_master enables material spend analytics, three-way match validation by SKU, and purchased component co']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier (vendor) who issued this invoice.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was approved for payment.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'The reference date from which payment terms are calculated, typically the invoice date or goods receipt date.']
  blocking_reason varchar [note: 'The reason why the invoice is blocked for payment, such as price variance, quantity variance, or quality issues.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the invoice is denominated.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any discount amount applied to the invoice, such as early payment discounts or volume discounts.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date recorded on the invoice document, which may differ from the invoice date or posting date.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The exchange rate applied for currency conversion if the invoice currency differs from the company currency.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which the invoice is posted.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the invoice is posted for accounting purposes.']
  freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The freight or shipping charges included in the invoice.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total invoice amount before taxes and adjustments.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was issued by the supplier. This is the principal business event timestamp for the invoice.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The unique invoice number assigned by the supplier. This is the externally-known business identifier for the invoice.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the procure-to-pay workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: parked|posted|blocked|cleared|cancelled|rejected|pending_approval — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'The type or category of the invoice document.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the invoice record was last modified.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total invoice amount after all taxes, discounts, and adjustments. This is the amount payable to the supplier.']
  payment_block_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the invoice is blocked for payment.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date the payment was made to the supplier.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment must be made to the supplier according to the payment terms.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The method by which payment will be made to the supplier.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'The reference number assigned to the payment transaction when the invoice is paid.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status indicating whether the invoice has been paid.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The agreed payment terms between the buyer and supplier, such as Net 30, Net 60, or 2/10 Net 30.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was posted to the financial accounting system.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'The purchasing group or buyer responsible for the procurement transaction.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'The organizational unit responsible for procurement activities related to this invoice.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Additional reference number or code provided by the supplier on the invoice document for their internal tracking.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount applied to the invoice, including VAT, sales tax, or other applicable taxes.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax code applied to the invoice for tax calculation purposes.']
  tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The tax jurisdiction or authority under which the invoice tax is calculated.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Status of the three-way match verification process comparing purchase order, goods receipt, and invoice.']
  tolerance_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the tolerance check comparing invoice amounts against purchase order and goods receipt amounts.']
  tolerance_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount by which the invoice differs from the expected amount based on the purchase order and goods receipt.']
  tolerance_variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage variance between the invoice amount and the expected amount.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'The WBS element for project-related invoices, linking the invoice to a specific project phase or deliverable.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of withholding tax deducted from the invoice payment.']

  Note: 'Supplier invoice document received from a vendor and processed for three-way match verification (PO–GR–Invoice). Captures invoice number, supplier invoice reference, invoice date, posting date, gross amount, tax amount, currency, payment due date, payment terms, invoice status (parked, posted, blocked, cleared), and tolerance check results. Serves as the SSOT for procurement-side payables within the procure-to-pay cycle.'
}

Table finance.commodity_category {
  commodity_category_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for commodity_category']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Commodity categories are mapped to GL accounts in SAP MM for automatic account determination. This FK links the spend taxonomy to the chart of accounts, enabling automatic GL account assignment during']
  parent_category_commodity_category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent category in the hierarchy. Null for top-level (L1) categories.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the commodity category. May align with UNSPSC (United Nations Standard Products and Services Code) or internal taxonomy.']
  category_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the commodity category scope, including typical materials, services, or products classified under this category.']
  category_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the category within the taxonomy structure. L1 represents top-level, L2 sub-category, L3 detailed category, etc.']
  category_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the category manager responsible for strategic sourcing and supplier relationships within this category.']
  category_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the commodity category (e.g., Electrical Components, Raw Steel, MRO Supplies).']
  category_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the commodity category within the procurement taxonomy.']
  compliance_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category has specific regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., RoHS, REACH, conflict minerals, ISO certifications).']
  contract_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category is covered by active procurement contracts or framework agreements.']
  cost_reduction_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual cost reduction target percentage for this category as part of strategic sourcing initiatives.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity category record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this commodity category classification became effective in the procurement taxonomy.']
  environmental_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category requires environmental compliance certifications such as ISO 14001, RoHS, REACH, or conflict minerals reporting.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this commodity category classification expires or is scheduled for review. Null for active categories without planned expiration.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity category record was last updated or modified.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this category was last reviewed for accuracy, relevance, and alignment with business needs.']
  lead_time_days_avg varchar [note: 'Average procurement lead time in days for materials and services within this category, from requisition to delivery.']
  moq_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether suppliers in this category typically enforce minimum order quantity requirements.']
  negotiation_frequency varchar [note: 'Typical frequency of contract negotiations and RFx events for this commodity category.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this commodity category classification and sourcing strategy.']
  preferred_supplier_count varchar [note: 'Number of preferred suppliers currently approved and active for this commodity category.']
  preferred_supplier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category has designated preferred suppliers with negotiated contracts or framework agreements.']
  price_volatility_index varchar [note: 'Classification of price volatility for commodities in this category based on historical price fluctuations and market dynamics.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Code identifying the purchasing group (buyer team) responsible for operational procurement within this category.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Code identifying the purchasing organization responsible for procurement activities within this category. Aligns with SAP organizational structure.']
  quality_certification_required varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of required quality certifications for suppliers in this category (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949, AS9100).']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this commodity category.']
  risk_classification varchar [note: 'Risk classification based on supply chain vulnerability, supplier concentration, geopolitical factors, and material criticality.']
  sourcing_strategy varchar [note: 'Defined sourcing strategy for this category: single-source, dual-source, multi-source, global sourcing, or local sourcing approach.']
  spend_type varchar [note: 'Classification of spend type: direct materials (production inputs), indirect materials (non-production), MRO (Maintenance, Repair, Operations), CapEx (Capital Expenditure), or services.']
  strategic_sourcing_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this category for strategic sourcing initiatives based on spend volume, supply risk, and business impact.']
  unspsc_class varchar [note: 'Third-level UNSPSC class name within the family (e.g., Cutting and Forming Machines).']
  unspsc_code varchar [note: 'Eight-digit UNSPSC classification code for global commodity standardization and cross-industry spend analysis.']
  unspsc_commodity varchar [note: 'Fourth-level UNSPSC commodity name, the most granular classification level (e.g., CNC Milling Machines).']
  unspsc_family varchar [note: 'Second-level UNSPSC family name within the segment (e.g., Metalworking Machinery and Equipment).']
  unspsc_segment varchar [note: 'Top-level UNSPSC segment name (e.g., Industrial Manufacturing and Processing Machinery and Accessories).']

  Note: 'Commodity and spend category taxonomy used to classify all purchased materials and services into a hierarchical structure aligned with UNSPSC or internal category codes. Captures category code, category name, category level (L1/L2/L3), parent category, spend type (direct/indirect/MRO/CapEx), category owner, preferred supplier flag, and strategic sourcing priority. Enables category management, spend cube construction, and sourcing strategy alignment.'
}

Table finance.cost_center {
  cost_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the cost center.']
  parent_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent cost center in the hierarchy.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual costs incurred by the cost center.']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate costs from this center to other entities.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned budget allocated to the cost center for the fiscal period.']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'Code of the controlling area to which the cost center belongs.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Business key used to identify the cost center in financial transactions.']
  cost_center_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the cost center\'s purpose and scope.']
  cost_center_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of cost centers for reporting or allocation purposes.']
  cost_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the cost center.']
  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center by functional area.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost center record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency in which the cost center\'s financials are recorded.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used to map the cost center to external systems or legacy codes.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the cost center within the organizational hierarchy.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Slash‑delimited path representing the cost center\'s position in the hierarchy (e.g., "/100/200/300").']
  is_overhead boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost center is used for overhead cost allocation.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the physical location or plant associated with the cost center.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department that owns the cost center.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost center record.']
  variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Difference between budgeted and actual costs.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date when the cost center becomes effective.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date when the cost center is retired or becomes inactive.']

  Note: 'Master record for all SAP CO cost centers representing organizational units responsible for incurring costs. Captures cost center hierarchy, responsible manager, controlling area assignment, profit center assignment, currency, validity period, cost center category (production, administration, R&D, sales), and standard hierarchy position. Serves as the SSOT for internal cost allocation, overhead absorption, and management accounting structures. The primary controlling object for budget assignment, actual cost collection, and variance analysis in manufacturing operations.'
}

Table finance.profit_center {
  profit_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the profit center.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Profit centers belong to a company code (legal entity) in SAP CO. The existing `legal_entity_code` STRING field is a denormalized reference to the company code; replacing it with company_code_id FK no']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Link to the primary cost center that supplies cost data for this profit center.']
  parent_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent profit center in the hierarchy, if any.']
  actual_profit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Realized profit for the profit center in the reporting period.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Approved budget amount for the profit center for the fiscal period.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Code of the controlling area to which the profit center belongs for internal cost accounting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the profit center record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code in which the profit center records its functional amounts.']
  financial_reporting_line varchar [note: 'Line of financial reporting hierarchy (e.g., segment, division, business unit) used for IFRS segment reporting.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating the profit center\'s depth in the organizational hierarchy.']
  is_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the profit center is included in statutory and management reporting.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent strategic review of the profit center.']
  oee_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness percentage assigned to the profit center for performance benchmarking.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Business unit or individual accountable for the profit center\'s performance.']
  planned_profit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Profit amount planned for the profit center at the start of the reporting period.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the profit center, often used in financial postings.']
  profit_center_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context about the profit center\'s purpose and scope.']
  profit_center_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping name used for consolidated reporting across multiple profit centers.']
  profit_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the profit center used in reports and dashboards.']
  profit_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the profit center.']
  profit_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification indicating whether the profit center is internal, external, or a joint‑venture entity.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the profit center primarily operates.']
  reporting_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency code used for external financial reporting of the profit center.']
  segment varchar [note: 'High‑level business segment classification for the profit center.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction applicable to the profit center.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the profit center record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the profit center record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the profit center record.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date on which the profit center becomes effective for reporting.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date on which the profit center ceases to be effective; null if still active.']

  Note: 'Master record for profit centers representing autonomous business segments within the manufacturing enterprise used for internal profitability reporting. Captures profit center hierarchy, controlling area, responsible manager, segment classification, currency, and validity dates. Enables P&L reporting at sub-entity level per SAP EC-PCA (Profit Center Accounting) aligned with IFRS segment reporting.'
}

Table finance.gl_account {
  gl_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the GL account record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: GL account may be assigned to a cost center for reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: GL account may be assigned to a profit center for reporting.']
  account_category varchar [note: 'Business‑level categorisation used for reporting and analysis.']
  account_group varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping code that aggregates multiple GL accounts for reporting.']
  account_long_description varchar [note: 'Extended description providing additional context for reporting and audit.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose of the GL account.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the GL account, used in postings and reporting.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the account by financial statement section.']
  balance_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account is a natural debit or credit account.']
  consolidation_group varchar [note: 'Identifier of the consolidation group for legal entity roll‑up.']
  cost_element_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account is linked to a primary or secondary cost element for CO integration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the GL account record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code of the currency in which the account is denominated.']
  current_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Running balance reflecting all posted transactions to date.']
  external_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source ERP or external system that created the account.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Organisational functional area to which the account belongs (e.g., Manufacturing, R&D, Sales).']
  gl_account_description varchar [note: 'Long textual description of the account purpose and usage.']
  gl_account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the account.']
  is_bank_account boolean [note: 'True if the account is linked to a bank account for cash management.']
  is_budget_enabled boolean [note: 'True if the account participates in budgeting processes.']
  is_cash_account boolean [note: 'True if the account represents cash or cash equivalents.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'True if the account is scheduled for retirement and should no longer be used for new postings.']
  is_expense_account boolean [note: 'True if the account is used for operating expense postings.']
  is_fixed_asset_account boolean [note: 'True if the account is used for fixed‑asset accounting.']
  is_forecast_enabled boolean [note: 'True if the account is included in financial forecasting.']
  is_income_account boolean [note: 'True if the account records income other than sales.']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'True if the account is used for intercompany transactions.']
  is_inventory_account boolean [note: 'True if the account tracks inventory valuation.']
  is_purchase_account boolean [note: 'True if the account records purchase expenses.']
  is_reconciliation_account boolean [note: 'True if the account is used for automatic reconciliation of sub‑ledger postings.']
  is_sales_account boolean [note: 'True if the account records sales revenue.']
  is_tax_relevant boolean [note: 'True if the account is subject to tax reporting requirements.']
  opening_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Balance of the account at the start of the fiscal year.']
  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for consolidated financial reporting.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business segment code (e.g., Industrial, Energy, Services) for segment reporting.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification applied to postings against this account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the GL account record.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which the account becomes effective for posting.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date after which the account is no longer valid for posting (nullable).']

  Note: 'General ledger account master record serving as the SSOT for the chart of accounts used across all financial postings. Captures account number, account type (balance sheet, P&L), account group, reconciliation account flag, currency, tax category, field status group, cost element category (primary/secondary for CO integration), functional area, and IFRS/GAAP classification. Bridges financial accounting and management accounting by carrying cost element attributes that enable controlling postings. Governs all financial postings and is the backbone of the enterprise chart of accounts.'
}

  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Controlling area assigned for cost accounting.']

  number_of_employees varchar [note: 'Total number of employees for the legal entity.']

  public_company_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the entity is publicly listed.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number of the entity with the corporate registry.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business segment classification for reporting.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the entity is tax-exempt.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (e.g., VAT, EIN) for the legal entity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the company code record.']

  Note: 'Master record for SAP FI company codes representing legally independent entities within the manufacturing group. Captures company code identifier, company name, country, currency, fiscal year variant, chart of accounts assignment, controlling area assignment, consolidation group, consolidation method, and elimination scope. Serves as the organizational anchor for all financial postings, statutory reporting under IFRS/GAAP, and the consolidation unit for group financial statement preparation.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the journal entry record.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: In SAP FI, AP invoice postings generate journal entries. Linking journal_entry back to the source ap_invoice enables full audit trail from GL posting to originating AP document. The existing `referenc']
  ar_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_item. Business justification: AR postings (customer payments, invoices, credit memos) generate journal entries in SAP FI-AR. Linking journal_entry to the source ar_item provides traceability from GL postings back to the originatin']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Every SAP FI journal entry is posted under a specific company code (legal entity). The existing STRING field `company_code` is a denormalized code; replacing it with company_code_id FK normalizes this']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Journal entry references a cost center; replace code with FK.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Asset acquisitions, depreciation runs, and disposals all generate journal entries in SAP FI-AA. Linking journal_entry to fixed_asset enables direct traceability from GL postings to the asset register,']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Journal entries post to GL accounts — this is the fundamental SAP FI relationship. Every journal entry line item references a GL account. Adding gl_account_id as the primary posting account FK enables']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: Journal entries for production order settlements, WIP postings, and variance postings reference the originating work order. In industrial manufacturing CO-PC, period-end settlement creates journal ent']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Journal entry references a profit center; replace code with FK.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit log entry for compliance tracking.']
  batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch for mass postings.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'Higher‑level business area used in financial reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the journal entry record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the transaction currency.']
  document_date date [note: 'Date on the original source document (e.g., invoice date).']
  document_header_text varchar [note: 'Header description of the journal document.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the journal entry, typically the SAP document number.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the journal entry indicating its purpose (e.g., invoice, credit note).']
  entry_date date [note: 'Date when the journal entry was entered into the system.']
  exchange_rate decimal(15,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert transaction currency to local company currency.']
  exchange_rate_type varchar [note: 'Indicator of the exchange rate calculation method (e.g., average, spot).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Numeric period (e.g., month) within the fiscal year for the posting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the posting belongs.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Variant of the fiscal year used for special reporting calendars.']
  gaap_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the posting complies with local GAAP reporting standards.']
  ifrs_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the posting complies with IFRS reporting standards.']
  is_adjusted boolean [note: 'True if the entry represents an adjustment rather than a primary posting.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of the posting converted to the company’s local currency.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount (debits minus credits) of the journal entry.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the journal entry was posted to the general ledger.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'SAP posting key indicating debit or credit nature of the line.']
  posting_period varchar [note: 'Period identifier used for period‑end closing.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the journal entry.']
  posting_text varchar [note: 'Free‑form text entered by the user describing the posting.']
  posting_time timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the entry was posted to the ledger.']
  reference_document varchar [note: 'External document number referenced by this journal entry (e.g., purchase order).']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the original entry that this entry reverses.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this entry reverses a previous posting.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Segment classification for reporting (e.g., Industrial, Services).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source ERP/system that generated the journal entry (e.g., SAP FI).']
  tax_amount_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate tax amount calculated for the posting.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applicable to the posting.']
  total_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of credit line amounts for the journal entry in local currency.']
  total_debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of debit line amounts for the journal entry in local currency.']
  transaction_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of the posting in the transaction currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the journal entry.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'WBS element reference for project accounting.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record capturing all general ledger postings at both document header and line item level, serving as the single source of truth for the complete financial audit trail. Header attributes include document number, document type, posting date, fiscal year, period, company code, currency, exchange rate, reference document, reversal indicator, and IFRS/GAAP compliance flags. Line item attributes include line number, GL account, debit/credit indicator, amount in transaction and local currencies, cost center, profit center, WBS element reference, internal order, tax code, assignment field, and posting text. Covers manual journal entries, automated system postings, accruals, reversals, reclassifications, and period-end closing entries. Foundation for trial balance, financial statements, reconciliation, and audit evidence.'
}

Table finance.budget {
  budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the budget record.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Finance budgets in SAP CO are always scoped to a company code (legal entity). This FK links budget records to the legal entity master, enabling entity-level budget vs. actual reporting, financial plan']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Budget is planned for a specific cost center; replace code with FK.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Budget can be linked to a GL account for accounting entries.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: Budgets are organized by plant in industrial manufacturing for plant-level cost planning, capital expenditure approval, and budget variance reporting. Finance controllers prepare plant budgets for ann']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Budget is planned for a specific profit center; replace code with FK.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Territory-level sales budget planning: industrial manufacturers set annual revenue budgets by sales territory for quota allocation, budget vs. actual variance reporting, and sales performance manageme']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the budget was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the budget.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the budget.']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the budget, used for reference and reporting.']
  budget_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the budget.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget version: original, revised, or supplemental.']
  cost_element varchar [note: 'Cost element classification for budgeting purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for budget amounts.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code of the department owning the budget.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the budget expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the budget becomes effective.']
  finance_budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit fiscal year to which the budget applies.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'Method used to generate forecasted amounts within the budget.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the budget is currently active.']
  is_capex boolean [note: 'Indicates if the budget pertains to capital expenditures.']
  is_opex boolean [note: 'Indicates if the budget pertains to operational expenditures.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent budget review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the budget.']
  period varchar [note: 'Reporting period covered by the budget (quarterly or annual).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget record was first captured in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the budget record.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code for the budget.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of budget review cycles.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of the budget data (e.g., SAP S/4HANA).']
  total_committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate amount that has been committed (e.g., purchase orders).']
  total_planned_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate planned amount for the budget across all periods and categories.']
  total_revised_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate revised amount after adjustments.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the budget record.']
  variance_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable variance percentage between planned and actual amounts.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the budget.']
  version_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget version was created or released.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the budget record.']

  Note: 'Budget planning and tracking records capturing both header-level governance and period-level line item detail for all controlling objects (cost centers, profit centers, internal orders). Header captures budget version, fiscal year, budget type (original, revised, supplemental), approval workflow status, responsible manager, and total planned amounts. Line items capture controlling object assignment, GL account, cost element, fiscal period, planned amount, revised amount, committed amount, currency, and budget category (CapEx, OpEx, headcount, materials). Supports FP&A variance analysis (plan vs actual vs forecast), budget-to-actual reporting, rolling forecast integration, budget transfer workflows, and CapEx/OpEx governance across the manufacturing enterprise.'
}

Table finance.ap_invoice {
  ap_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the accounts payable invoice record.']

  company_code_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.company_code']

The GL account determines the expense or liability account for the invoice posting. This FK enables direct navigation from AP invoice to the chart o']

FK enables GR/IR clear']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: AP invoice belongs to a profit center; replace string code with FK.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.purchase_order. Business justification: AP invoices are matched to POs for three-way match (PO/GR/Invoice) in industrial manufacturing — a core AP processing and audit compliance requirement. purchase_order_number is a plain-text denormaliz']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor that issued the invoice.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the invoice approval workflow.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the invoice was approved.']
  approval_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the invoice.']
  bank_statement_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of matching the payment against bank statement entries.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'Date used as the starting point for payment terms calculation.']
  cash_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied when payment is made within discount period.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the accounting document that clears the invoice.']

  discount_taken decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of any cash discount applied.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'External invoice number assigned by the vendor or the organization.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the invoice must be paid according to payment terms.']
  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes, discounts, and withholdings.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the vendor issued the invoice.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice (e.g., standard invoice, credit memo).']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount payable after taxes, discounts, and withholdings.']
  payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount actually paid for the invoice.']
  payment_block_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why payment processing is blocked for this invoice.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment was actually made.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to settle the invoice.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number provided by the bank for the payment transaction.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment execution.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard terms defining when payment is due (e.g., Net 30, 2% 10 Net 30).']
  payment_value_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment amount is considered effective.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was posted to the general ledger.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the invoice record.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax calculated for the invoice.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code that determines the tax rate and calculation rules applied.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is exempt from tax.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax percentage for the invoice.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Result of PO/GR/IR verification: matched, unmatched, or partially matched.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of withholding tax deducted at source.']
  withholding_tax_code varchar [note: 'Code defining the withholding tax rate and rules.']

  Note: 'Accounts payable full lifecycle records capturing vendor invoices and their associated payment execution processed as a unified payables document. Invoice attributes include document number, vendor, company code, invoice date, posting date, baseline date, payment terms, gross amount, tax amount, net amount, three-way match status (PO/GR/IR verification), payment block reason, and approval workflow status. Payment attributes include payment method (ACH, wire, check, direct debit), house bank account, value date, payment amount, discount taken, cash discount percentage, clearing document reference, payment run identifier, and bank statement reconciliation status. Covers the complete payables cycle from invoice receipt through three-way matching, approval, payment proposal, payment execution, and bank clearing. Serves as the SSOT for all vendor obligations, payment commitments, and outgoing cash flows.'
}

  warranty_provider varchar [note: 'Entity that provides the warranty coverage.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the asset warranty becomes effective.']

  Note: 'Fixed asset register and complete transaction history serving as the lifecycle record for all capitalized assets of the manufacturing enterprise including production machinery, CNC equipment, robotics, automation systems, buildings, vehicles, tooling, and IT infrastructure. Master data captures asset number, asset class, description, serial number, acquisition date and value, useful life, depreciation method/key, salvage value, location/plant assignment, and cost center assignment. Transaction history captures all asset movements: acquisitions, retirements, transfers, revaluations, impairments, and periodic depreciation postings with transaction type, posting date, amount, document reference, and depreciation area (book/tax/IFRS). Provides accumulated depreciation, net book value, replacement value, and insurance value. The SSOT for asset valuation, depreciation scheduling, capital base reporting, and insurance declarations.'
}

  Note: 'Master record for all house bank accounts used by the manufacturing enterprise for payment processing, cash pooling, and liquidity management. Captures bank key, account number, IBAN, SWIFT/BIC, bank name, country, currency, GL account assignment, payment method eligibility, daily transaction limits, and cash pool membership. Serves as the SSOT for treasury operations and bank statement reconciliation in SAP FI.'
}

Table finance.business_partner {

  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number of the organization (e.g., company registration).']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk assessment rating for the partner.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used name for the business partner.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system for the partner record (e.g., SAP, external CRM).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the business partner\'s primary address.']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC code for international transfers to the partner\'s bank.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner is tax-exempt (true) or not (false).']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identifier for the partner (e.g., EIN, VAT).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the partner record.']
  vat_number varchar [note: 'Value Added Tax registration number, if applicable.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the business partner.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for business_partner. Referenced by business_partner_id.'
}
